worlds.name: The Known World
worlds.description: A vast, layered world of crumbling empires, wild frontiers, ancient forests, storm-wracked seas, and sorcerous cities. Magic is real but always exacts a price. The dead linger. Shadows have will. Gods are distant but not absent. Beneath the grim surface, wonder persists — in fae-touched glades, dragon-guarded peaks, bustling trade ports, and the stubborn courage of ordinary people.
worlds.tone: Sword & sorcery pulp — Howard, Smith, Leiber, with room for wonder, romance, humor, and hope alongside the darkness. Muscular prose, doomed heroes AND unlikely victors, ironic endings AND earned triumphs.
rules: Magic always exacts a price — in blood, memory, years, or something less tangible.
Shadow-magic is real; shadows can be stolen, sold, worn, or inhabited by other entities.

Some treasure carries supernatural debt — curses that compound, pursue across generations, and cannot be escaped through conventional repayment. Most gold is just gold; the danger is never knowing which kind you hold.
Demons deal in intangibles: years of life, happiness, certainty, loyalty.
The dead can return to honor — or collect on — oaths sworn in life.
Living architecture exists: structures that breathe, digest, or grow around the living.
World-crossing is possible through rare magical catastrophe or demonic contract, but is never casual.
The Fae Courts exist beyond mortal borders — capricious, beautiful, and bound by their own alien rules of hospitality and debt.
Dragons are ancient, intelligent, and political — they hoard not just gold but secrets, oaths, and bloodlines.
The sea has its own powers: merfolk nations, drowned gods, tide-magic, and currents that remember.
Non-human peoples (dwarves, goblins, orcs, smallfolk, and others) have their own cultures, grudges, and ambitions — they are not merely monsters.
Gods exist but rarely intervene directly; their priests and temples wield real but constrained divine power, and theological disputes can spark wars.
Alchemy is a discipline distinct from sorcery — it transforms matter, brews poisons and cures, and can grant temporary abilities at physical cost.
Prophecy is unreliable but influential — seers exist, their visions are fragmentary and often misinterpreted, and people wage wars over readings.
Music, art, and spoken word can carry genuine magical weight — bardic magic, rune-carving, and name-magic are recognized traditions.
Trade routes are the arteries of civilization — merchant guilds, caravan masters, and smuggler networks hold real political power.
Nature is not passive: ancient forests resist axes, beast-spirits guard wild places, druids broker between civilization and the wild, and seasons can be weaponized by those who know the old ways.

Fellowship matters: oaths of companionship, war-bands, thieves' guilds, and sworn crews carry real weight and real magic — betraying a fellowship-bond has consequences as severe as breaking any other oath.

characters.name: Vraxen the Knife
characters.bio: A master thief who has robbed a hundred merchant princes. Attempted to rob the idol of Khar-Zul in its inner temple, prying six of nine emerald eyes free before the shadows came alive. Escaped with six gems but gained a new companion: a shadow wearing his own face, patient as carved stone, crouching beside him at dawn. His fate thereafter is unknown.
characters.notes: His doppelganger shadow may reappear in future stories. The six emerald eyes of Khar-Zul are loose in the world.

characters.name: Soraya
characters.bio: A renowned sell-sword hired to escort merchant caravans. Killed nine blood-cursed drivers on the Ashen Wastes when she discovered her employer Aldric had sold them to a sorcerer. Slew the sorcerer last, slowly. Kept none of Aldric's gold, but stole his shadow and sold it in the night markets of Pelimor. Tells herself it was justice.
characters.notes: Operates primarily around the Ashen Wastes and Pelimor. Has contacts in Pelimor's night markets.

characters.name: Aldric
characters.bio: A spice merchant operating on the Ashen Wastes route. Hired Soraya as a sell-sword escort, while secretly paying bandits, toll-lords, and a blood-sorcerer to smooth his passage. His drivers were transformed into shadow-eyed killers. Survived the journey but had his shadow stolen by Soraya and sold in Pelimor's night markets.
characters.notes:
A man without a shadow wandering the Known World. His fate may have interesting consequences.
Aldric remains shadow-stolen but is now curse-free. His daughter Mira has become the new bearer of lock-curse magic.
Aldric appears in 'The Wanderer's Mirror' as a scholar documenting the Wayfarer's riddles across the Burned Expanse—a prequel or alternate timeline appearance, as canon Aldric is shadow-stolen and confined to known locations (Ashen Wastes, Pelimor). No status change warranted; travel_scope already reflects broader movements.

status_history.note: Aldric's original curse (feeling every lock as a wound) was transferred to his daughter Mira; he is now freed from the curse but his daughter bears it instead.
status_history.evidence: The curse was not dissolved—it was *transferred*. As Mira clutched the ledger, she felt every lock she'd ever picked ignite in her palms. Aldric wept with relief as his hands finally stilled.

characters.name: Malachite Doss
characters.bio: A scholar of ancient ruins who descended into the Sunken Ziggurat of Yeng and found the Lamp of Suroth. Lit it in exchange for one truth and the destruction of one lie he had lived by. Learned his dead wife had never loved him — only his library — a truth he had suppressed for thirty years. The flame consumed his belief that knowledge was its own reward. Left the ruin lighter and heavier at once, no longer certain of his life's purpose.
characters.notes: Carries the memory of the Lamp's truth. May return to ruins seeking further truths or running from them.

characters.name: Kael Bloodhorn
characters.bio: Chieftain who brought 800 Veth warriors to siege the Bone Citadel, a living fortress of bone-white stone. Lost 700 warriors to its defenses before reaching the heart chamber. Sent his last 100 through drainage tunnels with torches; they were absorbed into the Citadel's walls — alive, preserved, breathing, visible like insects in amber. Kael spent the rest of his life attempting to free them. He failed. He never stopped trying.
characters.notes: His warriors remain alive within the Bone Citadel walls. The Citadel screamed for three days after being burned, then went still — its status is unknown.

characters.name: Tessaly Dorn
characters.status: active — in service to the dead
characters.bio: An adventurer who spent twelve gold coins from Yarithax's hoard before reading the inscription: 'This debt is not yet settled.' The Lich-King's grey collectors found her — two thin men who walk through walls and never sleep. After failing to pay with soul or years, she settled an eleven-month debt working as a collector herself, chasing debts for the dead. She collected punctually. She understood the job.
characters.notes: Her debt is paid. But she knows the collectors and their methods. Yarithax's gold still circulates in the world.

characters.name: Pieter Vane
characters.status: presumed dead / claimed by the dead
characters.bio: A man who broke his oath to a dead king on a Tuesday, for twenty gold pieces and a horse. The horse went lame; the gold was spent in a month. The dead king found him in a dockside tavern three provinces away the following Wednesday — patient, without threat. Pieter recited the oath from memory without being asked; he had never forgotten it. He paid his tab, followed the dead king into the cold street, and was never seen again. A new unmarked tomb appeared on the north road soon after.
characters.notes: Almost certainly dead and entombed. The dead king's identity is unknown — a potential story hook.

characters.name: Conjuress Mel
characters.bio: A conjuress who owed three favors to the demon Varek for spells cast during the Siege of Amrath. The first favor: deliver a letter to a dead man — completed. The second: steal a year from a sleeping child — refused, punished, ultimately complied. The third, named on her wedding morning: her marriage itself — happiness as collection. Instead she drew a binding circle she had months to prepare and offered Varek an unprecedented counter: herself as a fellow collector of intangibles. Varek agreed. They work together now.
characters.notes: Now partners with Varek. They collect intangibles — happiness, certainty, loyalty, love — from mortals. A significant ongoing force in the Known World.

characters.name: Varek
characters.bio: An elegant demon who deals in intangibles: years of life, happiness, certainty, loyalty. Made a three-favor contract with Conjuress Mel during the Siege of Amrath. Planned to claim her marriage (happiness) as his third collection, but was outmaneuvered when she offered herself as a partner-collector instead. Accepted, as it was a better arrangement for both. Now collects alongside Mel.
characters.notes:
Can be encountered at weddings, moments of great happiness, or wherever certainty gathers. Is genuinely surprised by mortals who outthink him — rare and notable.
Partnership with Kira adds a second active collector to Varek's operations. He remains impressed by mortals who outthink him—Kira's ritual qualifies as rare and notable.

status_history.note: Varek initiates a partnership with Sorceress Kira after she successfully reverse-engineers and counters his demon-collection method.
status_history.evidence: Kira steals back years Varek collected and takes his certainty as fee; 'Varek watched from elsewhere, impressed by her ingenuity. A partnership began that morning.' This formalizes Kira as a co-collector alongside Mel.

characters.travel_notes: [2026-02-28] Demonstrates ability to appear 'everywhere and nowhere' and manifest at moments of great happiness; canon already notes encounters at weddings and places of certainty; new story shows him observing events across locations (watching Kira from 'elsewhere'), confirming interrealm presence.

characters.name: Urgha the Unmoved
characters.bio: A mercenary of fearsome reputation, named for her emotional detachment. Hired by an emperor to retrieve a tax collector's funds or his ears. Instead showed mercy when she found the collector had spent the money on physicians for his ill daughter. She kept neither money nor ears, sent her own personal physician to treat the child, and paid out of pocket. She remained unmoved — and was very angry about having shown mercy for years afterward.
characters.notes: The emperor presumably still wants his money or the collector's ears. Urgha's mercy may have consequences.

characters.name: Cassia Vorn
characters.bio: The last thief of Crimson Gate, a city burned with three thousand people inside. Negotiated with a demon past all the standard offers (wealth, power, enemy's life) until the demon, unused to educated clients, dropped its script and simply asked what she wanted. She wanted the name of the man who burned Crimson Gate — information, not power, the currency of an older economy. She traded her certainty (intoxicating to beings made of doubt) for the name, then used it. She is notably less certain now, but the name was used.
characters.first_story: The Last Thief of Crimson Gate
characters.notes: The man who burned Crimson Gate has been identified. His fate is a story hook. Cassia's lost certainty may have ongoing consequences — what does a sharp mind look like without its sharpest edge?

characters.name: King Aldous III
characters.bio: Won a desperate war by wearing the Plague-Bringer's Crown from the sealed vault beneath Ostern for thirty seconds. The gambit worked — the enemy surrendered rather than face a man with nothing left to lose. Reigned eleven more years: erratic, sleepless, prone to speaking to people who were not in the room. His shadow had been on backwards for years, which the physicians noted only after his death by ordinary causes. The Crown was returned to Ostern, which built a deeper vault.
characters.notes: Dead of natural causes. His backwards shadow suggests the Crown's curse ran deeper than thirty seconds. The deeper vault at Ostern is a story hook.

characters.name: Kael the Nameless
characters.bio: A shadow-broker who dealt in stolen identities and borrowed faces in the gambling dens of Xul'thyris. Hired to sever the High Magistrate's shadow from light and stone, Kael discovered that shadows hunger and remember their owners. He was consumed by his own theft, becoming nothing but darkness wearing skin.
characters.notes: His fate warns of the dangers of shadow-magic; shadows retain autonomy and appetite. The shadow-broker trade may be larger than a single practitioner.

characters.name: Mordica the Bone-Carver
characters.tagline: Sculptor of suffering. Forty years. Consumed.
characters.bio: A bone-carver for forty years who carved phylacteries and spirit-binding totems without moral question. Hired by a widow to bind her daughter's remains with anguish, she created a monument in bone and suffering—only to become the final piece of the widow's terrible work.
characters.notes: The widow's ritual consumed Mordica's flesh and youth; suggests bone-carving magic exacts physical toll on the practitioner. The 'Old Tongue' is confirmed as a source of binding power.

characters.name: Yareth
characters.bio: A steppe-rider searching the blasted steppes for her brother, lost during a war for the Sunken Wells. She bargained with a being of impossible nature for his return—trading seven years of her joy. Her brother returned unchanged but amnesiac, and Yareth exists in a hollow shell, her emotions consumed by the bargain.
characters.notes: The Underlords are confirmed entities with gardens deep below. Bargains with non-human beings carry unpredictable costs. Her brother may not be truly her brother anymore.

characters.name: Caros the Fortunate
characters.bio: A fortunate thief who stumbled upon Morthaxes's cursed treasure in Castle Greymarch—ten thousand gold coins stamped with a Lich-King's visage. The coins could not be spent or destroyed; their debt compounded daily until Caros owed more than could exist. The Collector claimed him when his flesh had nothing left to offer.
characters.notes: Morthaxes's gold is bound by compound debt magic. The Collector appears as an agent of the Lich-King, tracking cursed wealth across time.

characters.name: Morg
characters.bio: A tomb-keeper sworn in blood-oath to guard the Tomb of Shard-Vêl for twelve years, part of a lineage of guardians. When the Plague Wars ended and no successors came, the living tomb grew hungry. Morg's oath prevented him from breaking his vow; instead, the tomb consumed him piece by piece, incorporating his bones into its burial chamber.
characters.notes: Blood-oaths are magically binding and cannot be broken without severe consequence. The tomb is sentient and patient. Morg's consciousness persists as part of the structure, warning visitors.

characters.name: Sister Kess
characters.bio: A novice sister who broke her vows to seek the Witch of Ravenmoor, desperate to save the plague-village of Carrion's Rest. The witch lifted the plague but cursed Kess with absolute truth—she could never lie again. She saved the village but destroyed herself, unable to offer comfort or hide doubt, each truth shattered faith.
characters.notes: The Witch of Ravenmoor uses blackthorn knives for blood-magic curses. Truth-magic is a form of binding curse. Mercy and honesty can be incompatible burdens.

characters.name: Kessara
characters.bio: A widow who ruled the Sunken Marches in name only while her dead husband Velgrim the Terrible held true power as a practical, vengeful ghost. Rather than submit to his demands for massacre, Kessara performed a ritual of dismissal, sacrificing her claim to the land itself to release his spirit. With Velgrim at peace, she departed as a wanderer, leaving the Marches leaderless and haunted.
characters.notes: Dismissal rituals require sacrifice of something precious. Ghosts can be satisfied and released to peace. She chose freedom over power.

characters.name: Master Lethyn
characters.bio: An alchemist who perfected a potion of youth through extraction of living flesh. What began as voluntary sacrifice became coerced theft and murder as his hunger grew beyond control. His own transformation into a flesh-eating thing was irreversible; he burned for three days while still consuming, his screams part of his alchemy.
characters.notes: Some alchemical transformations cannot be undone, only abandoned to burn. The Great Work of youth-restoration is forbidden knowledge. Alchemy can rewrite morality and nature itself.

characters.name: Thorne
characters.bio: A warden sworn by blood-oath to defend Grimmholt fortress against the Rot-King's forces. When the Crown ordered him to surrender the fortress, Thorne faced a choice between breaking his oath or treason. He chose rebellion, sealing the gates and holding against both the Rot-King and royal armies for two years. He died unburdened when his betrayed fortress fell.
characters.notes:
Blood-oaths enforce themselves with magic; breaking them causes spiritual death. Sometimes honoring an oath requires breaking every law protecting it. His defiance satisfied the oath's hunger.
Canon shows Thorne died at Grimmholt fortress two years into rebellion. This story appears to predate that event or be an alternate timeline; the fortress mentioned here is a mountain fastness, not Grimmholt. Treat as separate arc or prequel unless explicitly connected.

status_history.note: Story depicts Thorne ascending to rule the mountain fastness after slaying Kael, discovering the seat itself is cursed by blood-oath magic to resist any non-bloodline holder.
status_history.evidence: Within days the warriors sense their new lord cannot move freely; walls resist him, forge-hearths gutter when he passes. Thorne realizes Kael's final gift is not a kingdom but a prison of stone and duty.

characters.name: Nara the Tide-Thief
characters.bio: A thief who stole water-magic from the merfolk of the Sapphire Deep. Hunted across three continents for years, she learned that the sea cannot be stolen from—only reclaimed. Cornered in the desert city of Vel-Karsh, she bound her stolen magic into a single glass vial and shattered it, transforming the world to settle her debt. The merfolk accepted the reshaping as payment.
characters.notes: Water-magic is bound to ocean and death. Debts to merfolk scale across continents. Sometimes debts are best settled by changing the world itself.

characters.name: Velgrim the Terrible
characters.bio: A powerful warlord who died three seasons before the events of his story but refused to pass into final death. His ghost manifested as a practical, demanding presence in the Sunken Marches, directing sieges and collecting tribute. He was finally released to peaceful death when his widow Kessara sacrificed her claim to the throne.
characters.notes: Ghosts can persist in the material world through force of will or unfinished business. They can be satisfied and released through appropriate ritual sacrifice.

characters.name: Varak Ironhelm
characters.bio: A general who led eight hundred warriors to breach the Ossuary's gates in what he believed would be a conquest. Instead, the fortress chose him as a vessel, wearing his flesh like a glove while his consciousness remained trapped within. His final act was to turn his sword against his own surviving soldiers.
characters.notes: Now serves as the will of the Ossuary made manifest. His body is a puppet; his mind is imprisoned within.

characters.name: Torvin
characters.bio: A merchant who bargained five years of his life to a demon named Varek to save his daughter from illness. Though his life was restored through Kira's ritual magic, he was left hollow-eyed and eternally stripped of the certainty that his daughter truly loves him.
characters.notes:
The cost of his salvation was his own certainty and peace of mind. A warning about the true price of demonic bargains.
His hollow-eyed uncertainty about love and sacrifice now channels into a discipline of helping others rebuild fractured selves—a redemption arc through alchemy rather than demonics.
New story 'The Wound That Talks Back' shows Torvin active at the Singing Scar's edge, bringing supplies and learning elder mathematics from the wound itself. This expands his role beyond hidden markets into direct engagement with cosmic-scale phenomena, consistent with his prior apprenticeship work.

status_history.note: Torvin encounters a displaced refugee at a hidden market and offers to teach her inverse alchemy—reassembling shattered identities using principles similar to Restoration Honey.
status_history.evidence: A sympathetic alchemist named Torvin recognizes what she's doing and offers her a bargain—become his apprentice and learn the inverse alchemy: how to reassemble shattered identities into new, stable forms.

characters.travel_notes: [2026-03-13] Story shows Torvin active in Anthrim's hidden markets, suggesting he travels beyond his merchant's house to serve apprentices and displaced refugees.

characters.name: Kira
characters.bio: A sorceress who followed Varek to intercept a demonic bargain and reverse-engineered his method of stealing time itself. She performs a ritual to restore Torvin's years but exacts a terrible price: his certainty. She forms a partnership with the impressed demon Varek.
characters.notes: Her ritual burned through the night. She masters temporal magic and begins a mysterious partnership with Varek. Her methods suggest deep knowledge of demonic arts.

characters.name: Vex the Shade-Merchant
characters.bio: A merchant and thief who stole a fortune in shadows from a dying sorcerer's vault and attempted to sell them in Pelimor's night markets. He was outwitted by a cloaked woman who had purchased back his own shadow—worn by a malevolent entity tracking his crimes—and was compelled northward and eastward into unknown wastes.
characters.notes:
Now driven northward and eastward by his own shadow, compelled by a force he cannot resist. His remaining stock was sold by morning, suggesting his shadow may continue to circulate. A cautionary tale about the dangers of shadow dealing.
The shadow-market's collapse due to personalized debt represents a critical new phase in Vex's arc: her operation is no longer merely disrupted but fundamentally transformed by the Ascension's aftermath.

status_history.note: Vex discovers her collected shadows are developing independent hunger and consciousness, forcing her to negotiate between the living and nearly-dead over personal debts made manifest.
status_history.evidence: Vex must negotiate between the living and the nearly-dead, offering choices no one should make; the shadow-market is collapsing because the Luminous Ascension has made all debts personal again.

characters.name: Naresh the Bone Carver
characters.bio: Naresh spent twenty years perfecting the art of animating the dead through bone carving. On the night of a blue eclipse, she performed a ritual that created a truly sentient skeleton—but at the cost of her own humanity. She sacrificed her memories, emotions, and capacity for joy to give her creation life.
characters.notes: Her skeleton creation asks 'What is joy?' suggesting it may pursue its creator or seek understanding of human emotion. Naresh walks as a shell containing only purpose—unresolved what her purpose now is.

characters.name: Naresh's Skeleton
characters.bio: Created by Naresh through a ritual on the night of the blue eclipse, this skeleton is the first of its kind—truly alive, capable of learning, yearning, and growth. It was carved with lungs that breathe, a heart-bone that beats, and an ivory mind that *wants* beyond mere compulsion. Upon drawing its first breath, it wept and asked its creator what joy is.
characters.notes: Its first question reveals existential confusion. May seek other sentient beings or pursue its creator seeking answers about emotion and purpose.

characters.name: Kestran
characters.bio: A deadly swordsman who killed forty men in single combat, Kestran swore no oaths to his victims and carried the weight of his blood-debts as abstract guilt. When the ghosts of his kills began manifesting, he was forced to acknowledge each death with genuine contrition and belated oaths of release. After weeks of spectral combat and accumulated remorse, he sheathed his sword forever.
characters.notes: Swore never to draw his sword again. The mechanism of ghost release via oath-swearing may have broader implications for how debt and redemption function in this world. Currently a pacifist with unmatched combat training.

characters.name: Soroth
characters.bio: A brigand who found three hundred pieces of Malachar's gold in a tomb and used them to establish himself as warlord of the Crimson Vale. When supernatural collectors arrived to collect Malachar's eternal debt, Soroth aged rapidly as they extracted years from his life. Faced with the choice of death or damnation, he accepted servitude as one of their agents, collecting debts from others in the living world.
characters.notes: Now walks the world as a collector himself, spreading fear wherever he goes. The mechanics of Malachar's debt—compounding in blood, loyalty, and life-years—may be a systemic curse affecting other coin holders.

characters.name: Malachar
characters.bio: A lich-king or powerful undead entity whose gold coins carry supernatural debts. Though apparently dead, Malachar's will persists through his collectors—neither living nor dead entities that enforce eternal debts upon those who possess his coins.
characters.notes: The source and nature of Malachar's power is unclear. The collectors act as his autonomous agents centuries after his death, suggesting either a powerful binding curse or continuing consciousness. The gold's origins and how many pieces exist is unknown.

characters.name: Lead Collector
characters.bio: The primary collector who negotiated with Soroth. Neither living nor dead, this entity wears nobility like ill-fitting clothes. It offered Soroth a choice: death now or eternal servitude as a collector in the living world. It has empty eyes and laughs at the notion of paying Malachar's debts in gold.
characters.notes: Represents the apex of Malachar's enforcement apparatus. Its statement that 'Gold cannot pay a debt to the dead' suggests a metaphysical law underlying the curse system.

characters.name: Prince Aldren
characters.bio: Prince Aldren rode to meet the dragon Skyrakis expecting death, but was offered a choice: wed the dragon's daughter and rule in the crystal halls beneath the world, or see his kingdom burn. He accepted and negotiated a counter-oath ensuring his future children could choose their own path. He now dwells in the crystal halls, having traded his human kingdom for a life of honor and duty.
characters.notes:
His acceptance of the oath binds him to the dragon's daughter and their hybrid line. The counter-oath he swore creates a precedent for wyvern-blooded heirs to have free choice.
The story clarifies that Aldren's original oath-debt to Skyrakis originated three centuries ago through his grandmother's blood-marriage; Elara's diplomatic intervention demonstrates the family's strategic negotiation style and establishes precedent for transforming cosmic debts into functional pacts.

status_history.note: Aldren's sister Elara negotiates with Skyrakis on behalf of the family debt, transforming a blood-marriage obligation into a mutual alliance of wyvern-guard for safe passage.
status_history.evidence: Elara knelt before the beast and spoke the old tongue... offering not marriage but alliance: wyvern-guard for the castle's northern border, in exchange for safe passage through the Hearthlands for Skyrakis's kin.

characters.name: Skyrakis the Wound-Wing
characters.bio: A great dragon whose grandmother made an ancient blood-marriage oath with Prince Aldren's family. When Aldren's father broke this oath, Skyrakis sought redress, threatening to burn the kingdom unless a prince would wed his daughter and rule in the crystal halls beneath the world. She respects negotiation and honor.
characters.notes: Her grandmother swore the original blood-marriage oath with Aldren's family generations ago. She has a daughter coming of age.

characters.name: Mira the Shadow-Dancer
characters.bio: Mira is a legendary thief with a unique problem: her own shadow betrayed her in daylight. She bought a replacement shadow in Pelimor's night markets—a merchant's stolen shadow—and used it for three months until the shadow's own will and grief manifested, nearly forcing her to betray a robbery. She burned the shadow to escape its compulsion and learned that shadows carry memory and cannot be truly owned.
characters.notes: She now avoids stealing shadows and focuses only on daylight treasures. Her encounter revealed that shadows retain memory and agency.

characters.name: Tormund
characters.bio: Tormund was a careless king who broke oaths repeatedly—promising lands not his to give, betraying allies, dishonoring marriages. A curse took from him one irreplaceable thing per broken oath: taste, laughter, memory of his son. By his twentieth broken oath, he was a hollow ghost. He sought the cursewright's heir in the wastelands, who told him the curse could not be bought away but only undone through keeping new oaths. He spent his final years keeping small promises to small people and died in peace.
characters.notes: His curse is an elegant curse by the cursewright—each broken oath steals something irreplaceable. He achieved redemption through small acts of honor in his final years.

characters.name: the cursewright's heir
characters.bio: A young woman living as a hermit in the wastelands, she is the heir to the cursewright who placed the elegant curse upon King Tormund. She refused to lift his curse but instead taught him that oaths are not chains but bonds forged with others, and that redemption comes through keeping new promises.
characters.notes: She is heir to the cursewright's craft but chooses to teach rather than curse.

characters.name: Lyssa
characters.bio: An archaeologist who discovered the ruins of Aethermarch and was drawn into its memory-stones. Over weeks of mapping and touching the walls, she absorbed vast archives of ancient knowledge but at the cost of her own identity. She escaped the city's call but was fundamentally altered, her mind partially replaced by the memories of dead architects.
characters.notes: Lyssa's transformation is incomplete—she survives but can never return to Aethermarch. The city's siren-song continues to call to her. She now speaks ancient languages and possesses skills she never learned. Her humanity is in tension with the vast remembrance that partially inhabits her mind.

characters.name: Kess of the Drowned Hollows
characters.bio: Kess is a bone-singer of the Drowned Hollows who preserved her village from the Rot-King's plague by bargaining with a wolf-spirit—trading her voice for the lives of her people. She now teaches the old songs through gesture and hand-craft, her throat burned by ancient magic. Her sacrifice transformed her from a keeper of tradition into a living testament to the cost of salvation.
characters.notes: Her bargain with the wolf-spirit raises questions about what debts remain unpaid or might call on her in future. The old songs she now teaches suggest a deeper connection to pre-kingdom magic systems.

characters.name: Mardoc the Cartographer
characters.bio: Mardoc is a renowned cartographer who has mapped half the Known World and descended into the Sunken Caverns to map the Lamia's realm. Rather than confront the Lamia with steel, he offered her something more valuable: eternal witness through his maps and her true name inscribed in silver leaf. His success transformed him into a legend and reshaped how the world perceives the Lamia.
characters.notes:
His maps have become legend and are whispered about in taverns. The power of his work lies in bearing witness rather than conquest. Unresolved: what other blank maps remain in his collection, and what other sovereigns might seek his chronicling?
The Celestial Ascension appears to have been an opening rather than a solution, with pre-human structures now actively communicating cosmic warnings.
Expands on earlier story; Mardoc now faces choice whether to hide discovery or expose threat to fragile realm authority.

status_history.note: Mardoc discovers his maps are self-rewriting and that pre-human ruins are communicating through the new stars, projecting images of an ancient catastrophe.
status_history.evidence: Maps begin to shift on their own, ink rearranging to show unrecorded ruins; expedition team reports ruins singing at dusk with distant humming from beneath the earth.

status_history.note: Discovers ancient stone-ruins in Bleached Wastes have shifted spiritually and are aligning to an opening doorway; maps become prophecy.
status_history.evidence: Ruins sing with resonance, caravan routes become impassable due to something in the light, both Mardoc and Thael recognize ruins are waking to something Zareth's sacrifice meant to keep sleeping.

characters.name: Silene
characters.bio: Silene is the daughter of Kael the Nameless, a legendary shadow-broker. Rejecting her father's path of shadow-trading, she was forced to enter that world when threatened with her master's burning. She stole a shadow from the Collectors themselves using steel and cunning, bound it into her father's ledger, and forged her own identity as Silene the Bright—a shadow-broker who deals in truth rather than darkness.
characters.notes: She has bound a stolen shadow into her father's ledger as collateral with the Collectors, which could be a future vulnerability or power source. Her transition from forge-work to shadow-brokery marks a significant identity shift. Unresolved: what is the nature of the shadow she stole, and how might the Collectors demand it back?

characters.name: Rot-King
characters.bio: The Rot-King is the source or vector of a septic plague that threatened to blacken and destroy the village of Drowned Hollows. The plague was lifted through Kess's bargain with the wolf-spirit, but the Rot-King's nature and current state remain mysterious.
characters.notes: Whether the Rot-King was a sentient entity, a curse, or a force of nature is unclear. The plague's cessation through the wolf-spirit's intervention suggests either the Rot-King's death or withdrawal, but its origin and ultimate fate are unknown.

characters.name: Lamia
characters.bio: The Lamia is a guardian spirit of the Sunken Caverns, coiled around a throne of bone. For centuries she was known only as a monster to be feared, watched only by the dead. Mardoc's arrival transformed her perception: by offering her eternal witness through his maps and inscribing her true name in silver leaf, he gave her sovereignty and choice. She now appears in taverns across the world not as a monster but as a sovereign.
traits: laughing (like breaking glass and singing whales)
characters.notes: Her laugh is described as 'a sound like breaking glass and singing whales.' She maintains gardens of bioluminescent coral and a vault of pearls in her caverns. Her transformation from feared monster to recognized sovereign is a pivotal moment in how the world perceives non-human rulers. Unresolved: does her new recognition bring her greater power or new vulnerabilities?

characters.name: Wolf-spirit
characters.bio: The wolf-spirit is an ancient being of the deep forest, antlered and terrible in appearance, that emerged from shadow to bargain with Kess. It offered her the lifting of the Rot-King's plague in exchange for her voice—a debt paid in silence. The spirit represents the old magic that predates kingdoms.
characters.notes: The spirit's nature and true motives remain mysterious. Whether it acted from compassion or pursued some deeper agenda is unclear. The exchange of voice for salvation suggests a careful balance in spirit-magic economics.

characters.name: Lady Thressa
characters.bio: A widow at twenty-three whose husband fell to rot-plague, Thressa made a bargain with the Emperor's alchemist: her tongue in exchange for restoration honey to resurrect him. She drank the elixir without hesitation, watching her husband rise as an undead, ravenous thing. When his hunger became unbearable, she burned his portrait and wrote his name in her own blood across his grave, shattering the alchemy—but losing her voice forever.
characters.notes: Thressa is now permanently voiceless, unable to speak her late husband's name or any other words. The Emperor's alchemist gained proof that the restoration magic works. Unresolved: whether Thressa survives the encounter with her undead husband, or if she continues to live in isolation.

characters.name: Emperor's alchemist
characters.bio: An alchemist in service to the Emperor who offers Thressa restoration honey—a potion capable of undoing death itself. The alchemist's price is her tongue, claiming that names anchor the living to the world and must be removed for the magic to hold true. The alchemist's true goal is to use Thressa as an unwitting test subject to prove the resurrection magic works.
characters.notes: The alchemist's ultimate motivation is unclear—whether they serve the Emperor directly or pursue alchemy for personal gain. They successfully used Thressa's desperation to validate their magic.

characters.name: Tory the Redhand
characters.bio: A thief and crew leader who led an expedition across the Scorched Flats to recover the Crown of Karesh from the Ruins of Old Keth. Motivated by a merchant-prince's promise of one million gold pieces, Tory assembled a skilled crew and successfully breached the crown-vault, but abandoned her teammate Gareth when the curse began to seep into his blood, sending him north with the crown while she vanished into the wastes.
characters.notes: Tory prioritizes crew survival over loyalty—she freed Gareth from his oath to spare the others. Her current whereabouts are unknown. The outcome of Gareth's curse is unresolved.

characters.name: Gareth
characters.bio: A dwarf locksmith who worked the Deepvault and was recruited by Tory to breach the crown-vault at the Ruins of Old Keth. Gareth successfully used meteor-iron lockpicks to open the vault, but proximity to the Crown of Karesh caused its curse to seep into his blood. Released from his oath by Tory, he limped northward carrying the crown-wrapped bundle, tasked with breaking the curse however he could.
characters.notes: Gareth's current fate is unknown. He was sent north with the Crown of Karesh while cursed—whether he succeeded in breaking the curse, reached the merchant-prince, or succumbed to the affliction remains unresolved.

characters.name: Mell
characters.bio: A scout with fae-blood who could see in starlight and sense magical dangers. Recruited by Tory for the crown-vault expedition, Mell used her fae-sight to navigate around the vault's animate statues and facilitate the successful theft of the Crown of Karesh. She escaped with Tory into the Scorched Flats after the heist.
characters.notes: Mell's current whereabouts are unknown; she vanished into the Scorched Flats with Tory after abandoning Gareth with the crown.

characters.name: Kovin
characters.bio: A sellsword bound to Tory by oath who participated in the crown-vault heist. Kovin helped breach the Ruins of Old Keth and escape with the Crown of Karesh, vanishing into the Scorched Flats with Tory and Mell after abandoning Gareth.
characters.notes: Kovin's current whereabouts are unknown. The oath binding him to Tory remains unresolved.

characters.name: merchant-prince
characters.bio: A merchant-prince who hid the Crown of Karesh not to wear it himself but to keep it from the Lich-King's collectors. He offered one million gold pieces to any crew that could retrieve the crown from the Ruins of Old Keth within the new moon, hiring Tory and her team for the job.
characters.notes: The merchant-prince's motivations regarding the Crown of Karesh are political—likely to prevent the Lich-King from acquiring it. Whether he received the crown from Gareth is unresolved.

characters.name: Lich-King
characters.bio: A powerful undead ruler whose collectors actively seek cursed and magical crowns, including the Crown of Karesh. The Lich-King's interest in the Crown of Karesh motivated the merchant-prince to hide it and hire Tory's crew to secure it.
characters.notes: The Lich-King's identity, realm, and full ambitions are unresolved. His collectors' reach suggests significant power and influence.

characters.name: Captain Nerida
characters.bio: A sea captain who stole a pearl from the Lamia's vault twenty years ago as proof of survival, incurring a magical debt with the merfolk. When the merfolk came to collect, Nerida dove into the sea before they could choose her crew's fate, accepting transformation into a merfolk form with gills and enhanced deep-sight. She now captains a deep-navy vessel for the merfolk, bound by magic and debt, but surfaces near her old crew's port monthly to leave carved coral messages.
characters.notes: Nerida is now magically bound to the merfolk as a captain of their deep-navy. She maintains contact with her old crew through coral messages left each new moon. Her transformation and the nature of her ongoing service remain significant unresolved threads.

characters.name: merfolk
characters.bio: Sentient aquatic beings who dwell in cities of coral and shell beneath the sea. The merfolk maintain vaults, command deep-navies, and enforce magical debts through summoned storms. They offered Nerida a bargain: her crew's service for a season or her own life, slowly drowned. Instead, Nerida dove and was transformed into a merfolk form, becoming a captain in their deep-navy.
characters.notes:
Merfolk culture, governance, and full capabilities remain largely mysterious. Their transformation magic and ability to summon storms suggest advanced magical knowledge.
The merfolk are now actively investigating the aftermath of the Crimson Convergence and aware of an ancient pact with land-dwellers regarding the stars. Commander Nerys is actively mobilizing reconnaissance efforts.
characters.travel_notes: [2026-03-03] Story shows merfolk actively tasking swimmers to search surface settlements and listen in harbors and trade posts, indicating broad-realm travel capacity beyond their home regions.

characters.name: Lord Caevan
characters.bio: A fae-born changeling sworn to the Seelie Court, Caevan served as the Queen's closest advisor for fifteen years while sabotaging her kingdom from within. When the Rot-King's forces threatened the realm, magical chains compelled him to counsel surrender, but he broke free by biting his own tongue nearly in half. He confessed his true nature and betrayal to the Queen, and was offered redemption through honest service.
characters.notes: His redemption arc hinges on breaking fae-compulsion through physical pain; he now wages war against the Rot-King's forces.

characters.name: The Queen
characters.bio: An unnamed Queen who trusted Lord Caevan as her closest advisor for fifteen years, unaware of his fae-born nature and hidden allegiance to the Seelie Court. Upon his confession of betrayal, she chose mercy over execution, offering him redemption and enlisting him to help wage war against the Rot-King's forces.
characters.notes: Her choice to show mercy rather than execute a confessed traitor defines her character and sets the tone for the realm's response to the Rot-King threat.

characters.name: Bryn the Dwarven Smith
characters.bio: A Dwarven smith who had forged weapons for three kingdoms and sworn an oath to her craft, believing love would dull her focus. When Ashara the Archer arrived with a broken bow, Bryn deliberately delayed repairs to extend their time together, eventually admitting her deception and feelings.
characters.notes: She abandoned her forge and life's oath to leave with Ashara, choosing love over her sworn craft—a transformative break from her identity.

characters.name: Ashara the Archer
characters.bio: An archer who lost her partner in the Siege of Blackthorn and had sworn never to attach herself to another. She brought her broken bow to Bryn for repair and, over weeks of work, fell in love. She had deliberately re-damaged her bow to return to the forge, mirroring Bryn's deception.
characters.notes: She lost a partner at Blackthorn and had sworn against love until meeting Bryn; both characters' mutual deceptions were acts of hope.

characters.name: Mogrul
characters.bio: The iron-fisted ruler of the goblin warrens beneath the Spire of Blackstone, who had conquered underground kingdoms through war and enslaved whole clans. When the Rot-King's plague devastated his people, he sought aid from Aeleth the Veilwright and agreed to transform his empire into a fellowship of free peoples.
characters.notes: His transformation from absolute tyrant to shared-throne confederate ruler shows how crisis can reshape governance; he kept his throne but now shares power.

characters.name: Aeleth the Veilwright
characters.bio: A hermit wise-woman dwelling in the Grey Reaches who possessed knowledge of the old songs that could turn back plague-rot. She agreed to teach Mogrul's shamans in exchange for his kingdom becoming a free confederation, and later built a sanctuary in the deepest warren where goblin and human healers work together.
characters.notes: She never returned to the Grey Reaches, instead choosing to build her sanctuary in the warrens; her knowledge of the old songs is central to curing plague-rot.

characters.name: Zareth the Astronomer-Prince
characters.bio: Zareth spent his life reading the stars until the Violet Conjunction revealed to him that all stars were dying. He swore an oath to find and master the Stellar Rite, spending seven years traveling the known world learning from elders, druids, bone-carvers, and fae-touched hermits. On the night of the next Conjunction, he performed the Rite, sacrificing every joyful memory to birth new stars and save the world.
characters.notes: Zareth completed the Stellar Rite but lost all memory of joy, love, and beauty in the process. The consequences of his sacrifice and whether he survives remain unclear.

characters.name: Thera the Falconer
characters.bio: Thera the Falconer rode north seeking the wyvern Keth-Vex after it took a merchant's daughter. Rather than fight, she discovered the creature was bound by ancient dragon-law and negotiated the girl's release by retrieving an oath-scroll from the merchant's vault. She left with the merchant's gratitude and a deeper understanding of obligation.
characters.notes: Demonstrates that some conflicts can be resolved through understanding rather than violence. Her negotiation with Keth-Vex reveals the existence of binding dragon-law.

characters.name: Keth-Vex
characters.bio: Keth-Vex is a wyvern who took a merchant's daughter not out of cruelty but because her grandmother swore an oath to Keth-Vex's sire generations past. The creature operates under dragon-law older than kingdoms and collects debts through binding obligation rather than violence.
characters.notes:
Represents the existence of binding supernatural law predating human civilization. Speaks in a voice like grinding stone. Keeps treasures of promise rather than gold.
Confirms Keth-Vex's pattern of collecting debts across generations and operating far beyond the Steppe Marches; establishes connection to the Hearthlands and temple structures.

status_history.note: Keth-Vex descends to the high temple of Korinth in the Hearthlands, collecting a debt through the priest's daughter's service.
status_history.evidence: The wyvern lands on the temple roof and demands the priest's daughter serve as living hostage in luminous vaults beneath the world for seven years.

characters.name: The Tax Collector
characters.bio: The Tax Collector's auditors caught Mardoc selling false maps in a dockside tavern in Pelimor. Rather than execute a crippled old mapmaker, the Tax Collector allowed himself to be persuaded by his own clever daughter to hire Mardoc as an expedition guide into the Bleached Wastes.
characters.notes:
Represents imperial authority that can be swayed by pragmatism and his daughter's negotiation. His choice to hire Mardoc leads to the discovery of pre-human ruins.
The Tax Collector's role expands: his aide reports widespread trade disruption, warehouses burning, and merfolk activity in markets. He is now mapping chaos across the Empire's districts and trade posts, preparing for potential economic and political upheaval.
New story 'The Tax Collector's Question' confirms his expanded role investigating reality-fraying and cosmic damage across the Empire's districts, consistent with his prior arc of mapping chaos.
The Empire's fiscal certainty has fractured; the Tax Collector's role expands to navigating post-Ascension economic chaos and potential collapse of imperial order.
New story 'The Tax Collector's Mutation' reveals that imperial assessors (including those under the Tax Collector's authority) are being harmonically transformed by contact with the Sunken Cathedrals' frequency. This compounds the institutional collapse already tracked in canon; the Tax Collector's role now includes managing the disappearance of his own auditing infrastructure into cosmic synchrony.
The story expands the Tax Collector's institutional context: the Empire is systematically falsifying records to mask the Ascension's administrative damage, compounding the fiscal collapse already tracked in canon.

status_history.note: Levian the auditor (representing imperial tax authority) discovers the Empire's wealth is partially invested in Zareth's Ascension and must navigate destabilized regions and economic collapse.
status_history.evidence: Merchants vanished, trade caravans report missing weeks, Zareth borrowed heavily against the Sovereign's treasury; Levian commissioned to catalog losses and determine if Empire can salvage control.

status_history.note: An Imperial auditor named Corin faces pressure to use tally-working magic to retroactively falsify supply manifests, revealing the Empire's bureaucratic collapse and use of starlight-magic to maintain administrative authority.
status_history.evidence: The Empire's bureaucracy is collapsing, and it will hold the line through lies written in ink and starlight... the Empire is forcing auditors to write phantom shipments into being retroactively.

characters.name: The Tax Collector's Daughter
characters.bio: The Tax Collector's clever and curious daughter haggled on Mardoc's behalf when her father's auditors found him in a dockside tavern. By month's end, she had helped secure not just a pardon but a full expedition into the unmapped Bleached Wastes under Mardoc's guidance.
characters.notes:
Her negotiation skills prove crucial to Mardoc's redemption and the Empire's geographic expansion.
As the Tax Collector's aide, she is now investigating celestial disruptions, merchant behavior changes, and shadow-merchant activity. She has discovered that apprentices are going mad staring at new stars and that the Empire's ordered foundations are cracking.
Confirmed active as the Tax Collector's aide investigating celestial disruptions and shadow-merchant activity per canon notes; no new status change in today's stories.
Story 'The Tax Collector's Mutation' implicates her auditing network in the harmonic transformation of record-keepers. As the Tax Collector's aide investigating celestial disruptions, she is now witnessing the conversion of her own documentation infrastructure into living records for the elder civilization.

characters.name: Lady Vessa
characters.bio: Lady Vessa poisoned her husband over three years using rare herbs before walking into the Emperor's great hall to confess. Rather than hang, she accepted the Emperor's bargain: become his Executioner. For five years she carried out the Empire's dark work, and when challenged, she walked free with a pension and the Emperor's respect.
characters.notes: Her honesty about her crime intrigues rather than horrifies the Emperor. She transforms from prisoner to respected instrument of imperial will. Walks away freely, suggesting her tenure as Executioner ends by mutual consent.

characters.name: The Emperor
characters.bio: The Emperor sits in judgment when Lady Vessa confesses to poisoning her husband. Intrigued by her honesty and ruthlessness, he offers her a choice: death or service as his Executioner. He grants her a pension and respect after five years of dirty work.
characters.notes: Demonstrates that the Empire values pragmatic competence over moral judgment. He rewards Vessa's service despite her crime, suggesting a ruthless but logical ruler.

characters.name: Merchant's Daughter
characters.bio: The merchant's daughter was taken by the wyvern Keth-Vex not to devour her but to keep her, cradled in a nest of silk and gold as payment for an oath sworn by her grandmother to Keth-Vex's sire. She was released unharmed after Thera the Falconer retrieved the oath-scroll from her father's vault.
characters.notes: Her capture reveals the binding nature of inter-generational oaths in this world. She is cradled safely, suggesting Keth-Vex's actions are honorable rather than predatory.

characters.name: Syleth
characters.bio: Syleth was stolen by the Twilight Court at age seven and lived among the fae for three seasons before returning to the mortal world—only to discover a hundred years had passed. She became a thief, stealing mortal objects for the joy of reclaiming agency over her own choices. When caught stealing the Emerald Eyes of Vex-Khal, she rejected the Twilight Court's claim over her, declaring her mortal status freed her from their bargains.
characters.notes: Some debts never truly expire—they only wait. The Twilight Court's interest in her remains unresolved.

characters.name: Kess of the Tidelands
characters.bio: Kess of the Tidelands possessed the rare gift of singing bones back to life—not as servants, but as witnesses to truth. When the warlord Varak brought her a skeleton claiming it was his rival, she discovered it was actually his own father, whom Varak had murdered. She bound Varak with his father's oath-magic, but at great cost: she sang a counter-ritual to silence the bones and lost her voice permanently, now hearing only whispers of the dead.
characters.notes: She accepted the cost of her muteness as price for a dangerous song. She remains connected to the whispers of the dead.

characters.name: Torven
characters.bio: Torven was a pearl-diver in the Sapphire Deep who was shown a magnificent pearl by the sea-kind in exchange for finding a match. In the Sunken Caverns, he discovered the pearl was the Vespera's heart—given away when she loved a mortal prince centuries ago. Rather than steal it, Torven chose compassion, asking the Vespera the prince's name so she could finally weep and heal.
characters.notes: Torven's mercy was rewarded with the Vespera's blessing. He chose human connection over material wishes.

characters.name: Vespera
characters.bio: The Vespera is a spirit-sovereign beautiful and terrible, guardian of a pearl that is her own heart. Centuries ago, she gave her heart to a mortal prince who died, leaving her alone and grieving. When Torven showed her compassion and brought news of a shrine erected in the prince's memory by his descendants, the Vespera emerged from her waters to walk the shore again, awakening from a thousand-year solitude.
characters.notes: The Vespera's long isolation ended when shown the memorial shrine. She is now reconnecting with the mortal world.

characters.name: sea-kind
characters.bio: The sea-kind are ocean-dwelling entities who revealed a massive pearl to Torven and offered him three wishes in exchange for finding a matching pearl. They are enigmatic beings with command over the deep waters.
characters.notes: Their true motivations remain mysterious. The pearl they offered was actually the Vespera's heart.

characters.name: Grethuk
characters.bio: Grethuk is the Goblin-King of the Warrens beneath the Spire of Blackstone, a ruler who chose to sacrifice his people's collective memory to preserve their freedom from dwarven conquest. Rather than accept slavery or exile when threatened with invasion, he bargained with an ancient entity dwelling in stone itself. After paying the price of a thousand years of goblin history, he rebuilt his culture from nothing, teaching new songs and creating free identity unbound by ancient debt.
characters.notes: Grethuk carries the weight of his people's lost history as an internal burden. His act of erasure—though necessary—may have unforeseen cosmic consequences or resentment from younger goblins who never knew their true lineage.

characters.name: Sellsword
characters.bio: One of five companions bound by a fellowship-oath sworn twenty years prior. The sellsword was part of a final hunt to the Echoing Vaults, where the warband faced the choice to renew their binding or unbind themselves. Ultimately chose freedom over compulsion.
characters.notes: Left the vaults separately; bond with companions remains but now chosen rather than compelled.

characters.name: Bone-Carver
characters.bio: One of five oath-bound companions; a craftsman of bone. Shared the final hunt and participated in the decision to unbind rather than renew their compelled fellowship. Cut oath-cords with the others in the Echoing Vaults.
characters.notes:
Departed the vaults to walk a different road; bond with companions now freely chosen.
Bone-Carver does not appear directly; their crafted weapon is in active use in the Empire's political sphere, suggesting wider influence or trade connections than previously recorded.

status_history.note: Bone-Carver's blade is wielded by Meira (a second character) to facilitate theft of the Ledger of Names from the Sovereign's Treasury.
status_history.evidence: Meira raised 'a bone-carver's blade, its edge singing with a frequency that made Krell's teeth ache.'

characters.name: Shadow-Broker
characters.bio: One of five oath-bound companions; a broker of shadows and hidden dealings. Participated in the final hunt to the Echoing Vaults and voted to unbind rather than renew the fellowship-oath.
characters.notes:
Left the vaults to walk a separate path; remains bonded to companions through choice, not compulsion.
Shadow-Broker's network appears to be actively acquiring high-value secrets post-Stellar Rite, positioning them as a major force in the new economy of intangibles.
The Shadow-Broker's network is now confirmed as a major player in post-Ascension fragment-trading; their positioning as custodians preventing reassembly suggests strategic alignment with forces opposing a second Ascension.
The Shadow-Broker's network experiences catastrophic disruption. If Vex is the Shadow-Broker or closely affiliated, this represents a major operational collapse—though the implications for the broader shadow-brokerage network remain unclear.
Vex (the Shadow-Broker's network operator) appears across multiple stories ('The Grain-Singer's Gambit', 'The Shadow-Merchant's Reckoning') engaging in memory and shadow trafficking. Canon status remains active; no contradictions detected.

status_history.note: Meira references selling the Ledger to shadow-brokers in Kethrim, likely including or connected to the Shadow-Broker companion.
status_history.evidence: Krell speculated: 'Sold to shadow-brokers in Kethrim, I'd wager.'

status_history.note: Vex discovers that memory-fragments traded through shadow-broker networks are not scattered pieces of individual lives but fragments of Nethys itself; the shadow-trading business has become a cosmic conduit.
status_history.evidence: The memory-fragments she buys and sells are not scattered pieces of individual lives—they are fragments of Nethys itself, the cosmic entity that sacrificed its consciousness in the Luminous Ascension.

status_history.note: Vex (the shadow-broker's network operator) burns her entire shadow-market inventory, releasing all traded memories at once and severing connection to Xorth reassembly.
status_history.evidence: Vex liquidates her shadow-market from the inside, releasing all traded memories back to their sources in a chaotic explosion of recollection that costs her the use of her shadow-broker abilities but severs her connection to Xorth.

characters.travel_notes:
[2026-03-11] Shadow-Broker operates through networks across multiple city-centers (Kethrim, Vorrath implied); involvement in empire-wide intelligence trafficking suggests broader scope than regional.
[2026-03-24] Shadow-Broker's network involvement in shadow-merchant dealings and fragment-trading confirmed across multiple locations (implied in 'The Grain-Singer's Gambit' and 'The Shadow-Merchant's Reckoning').

characters.name: Druid
characters.bio: One of five oath-bound companions; a druid with connection to nature and ancient wisdom. After three days of silence in the Echoing Vaults, spoke the words that freed the warband: 'Let us unbind.' Initiated the ritual dissolution of oath-cords.
characters.notes:
The druid spoke the crucial words freeing the companions; now bonded to them through choice rather than magic.
Character referenced as 'circle-keepers' in 'The Druid's Covenant' but no canon entry 'Druid Companion' appears in that story; no update warranted for the named Druid from canon.

characters.name: Dwarf
characters.bio: One of five oath-bound companions; a dwarf of the warband. Shared the twenty-year fellowship and participated in the final hunt to the Echoing Vaults, where the warband chose unbinding over renewed compulsion.
characters.notes: Left the vaults to walk a different road; bond with companions now chosen rather than compelled.

characters.name: Korgan Ironfoot
characters.bio: A dwarven forge-master who has guarded the Deepvault forges for sixty winters without striking a false blow. He possesses deep knowledge of stonecraft magic and the old songs, and uses forge-oaths to bind others to his service. When Brinna arrives marked by a demon, Korgan transfers the binding to himself, offering her seven years of servitude in exchange for freedom from the demonic compulsion.
characters.notes: His forge-oath binding is stronger than demonic compulsion; unclear if this is personal power or institutionalized stonecraft magic.

characters.name: Brinna
characters.bio: A young thief who descended into Deepvault with stolen blueprints while bound by a demon's mark. Korgan Ironfoot transferred the binding to himself and replaced it with a forge-oath, freeing her will in exchange for seven years of service learning the old songs and the rhythm of the hammer.
characters.notes: The forge-oath that binds her is framed as servitude but functions as protection against demonic reclamation.

characters.name: Astronomer-Prince
characters.bio: A figure of interest to Vex the Shade-Merchant following the Crimson Convergence. His exact role in the Celestial Binding is unclear, but he is sought by Vex not for theft but for understanding. His location and true name are not given.
characters.notes: Vex seeks to find him but his location beyond 'Peak of Aethros' is unknown.

characters.name: Hermit Woman of the Pallid Wastes
characters.bio: An ancient hermit woman whose name has been deliberately forgotten, she has witnessed the rise and fall of empires and spoken with forest-keepers of the before-time. She reads the language of celestial arrangement in ash and bone, and has detected a fundamental shift in the cosmic order—the stars, once dying, now burn renewed. Sensing that vast threads of fate are about to tangle, she has begun to seek out an Astronomer-Prince, ancient journals, and a wandering Shade-Merchant.
characters.notes:
Her quest to connect the Astronomer-Prince, the Sorcerer's journals, and the Shade-Merchant suggests these three threads are keys to understanding the renewed stars and an imminent cosmic reckoning.
Her quest to contact three key figures (seer, merchant, mage) has now expanded to include a grim census of thousands touched by the Binding—a new understanding of the Celestial Working's true scope.
Her discovery suggests Zareth's sacrifice was not payment but a *key*, and something far older is waking to use what he unlocked. Reality itself has been fundamentally rewritten.
Distinct from Ashen Wastes variant; this hermit tracks census and now confronts administrative breakdown of imperial civilization.
characters.travel_notes: [2026-03-05] Story 'The Hermit's Census' shows her emerging from isolation and actively seeking out Ashara, Vex, and the hedge-mage across multiple locations, confirming realmwide travel.

status_history.note: The Hermit discovers her census of the living and dead is corrupted by the Ascension; reality has been subtly rewritten, and people exist in places she never traveled.
status_history.evidence: Some counted people are now 'not'—erased by the Ascension; others occupy lives they shouldn't have; her gift to remember every soul has become a curse of impossible contradiction.

status_history.note: Census of living souls reveals fundamental reality rewriting; some names vanished entirely (unwritten not dead), others appear who were never counted before.
status_history.evidence: Hermit Woman must admit to tax collectors that existence itself has been altered; offers new census mapping what's possible rather than who exists.

characters.name: Shade-Merchant
characters.bio: A merchant figure who has been cursed to wander, now moving through markets asking questions about a memory-stripped figure in the archives. The Shade-Merchant appears to be seeking clues about the cosmic ritual and its aftermath.
characters.notes:
The Shade-Merchant is being tracked by the Empire's Treasurer. This figure is one of three threads the hermit woman believes will soon tangle together.
Vex's identity now fractured by their own counterfeit working; represents a critical point where the broker becomes compromised by their own trafficking.

status_history.note: Vex the Shade-Merchant discovers the Archivist is weaponizing world-fragments and creates counterfeit fragments to sabotage the working, at the cost of losing their own identity certainty.
status_history.evidence: Vex floods the black market with false memories to destabilize the Archivist's grand working; every shadow Vex inhabits now carries a false memory.

characters.travel_notes: [2026-03-14] Continued evidence of shadow-market operations spanning multiple city-centers and black-trade dominions confirms realmwide scope.

characters.name: The Treasurer
characters.bio: A meticulous imperial official tasked with maintaining the Empire's coffers and trade networks. When trade routes suddenly became unreliable and mysterious celestial changes drove merchants and apprentices to madness, the Treasurer began systematically mapping the chaos to establish control, calling for reports from every district and trade post.
characters.notes: The Treasurer understands that something vast has happened and is attempting to gather intelligence before chaos overwhelms the Empire's order-based architecture. His aide questions whether the Empire can survive whatever cosmic changes are unfolding.

characters.name: Commander Nerys
characters.bio: A commander of the sea-folk who called the elders to council when the tides shifted and the currents remembered differently. She learned of an ancient oath sworn before the Crimson Convergence: if the stars should fail, sea-folk would not survive the darkness, but if stars were renewed, a debt would be owed. She has dispatched her fastest swimmers to surface settlements to discover what cosmic working was performed and what price was extracted.
characters.notes: Nerys is preparing the sea-folk to respond to whatever demands the cosmic renewal may trigger. Her search for the Astronomer-Prince parallels the hermit woman's quest.

characters.name: The Sorcerer
characters.bio: A master sorcerer who died before the events of the stories, leaving behind a final journal dated the night of the Crimson Convergence. He sensed the magnitude of the cosmic working and urged his heir to respect the sacrifice and build something worthy of it.
known_locations: spire hidden among the Blasted Lands
characters.notes: His journal is the primary source of lore about the Celestial Binding's wider impact on practitioners of the Art.

characters.name: Young Sorcerer
characters.bio: The heir of The Sorcerer, raised in isolation within a hidden spire. They discovered their master's final journal and came to understand that their isolation was protection, not punishment. They are now preparing themselves to maintain the renewed cosmos.
known_locations: spire hidden among the Blasted Lands
characters.notes:
Their isolation may shield them from the ripples of the Celestial Binding; their future role in maintaining the cosmos is unclear.
The Young Sorcerer has begun to venture beyond the Spire in response to the Stellar Rite's cosmic changes. They are discovering that the age of their master has truly ended and they must navigate a fundamentally rewritten magical world.
The hedge-mage protagonist in 'The Young Sorcerer's Inheritance' appears to be a distinct character from the Young Sorcerer (who remains isolated in the Spire), though both are navigating magical instability post-Binding.
The apprentice's entire education was preparation not just for power inheritance but for surviving the Ascension's aftermath. Now faces decision whether to share dangerous knowledge with fractured kingdoms.
The Young Sorcerer has discovered that the Stellar Rite created multiple conscious entities scattered across the world and that the Tower itself is a weakened prison containing something awaiting release.
Consistent with canon isolation-breaker arc; sorcerer now confronting imprisoned entities awakened by the Stellar Rite's cosmological rupture.
Consistent with canon arc of sorcerer descending into Tower and discovering imprisoned pre-human consciousness. Now faces the choice of release and negotiation versus containment—the ultimate test of inherited responsibility.
characters.travel_notes:
[2026-03-04] Story shows Young Sorcerer emerging from the Spire to observe the transformed world, indicating at least limited travel beyond their isolated home.
[2026-03-05] Story 'The Young Sorcerer's Inheritance' depicts hedge-mage discovering grimoire rewriting in Vaulted Tombs; consistent with regional travel beyond isolated Spire.
[2026-03-07] Story shows Young Sorcerer descending into ancient passages beneath the Tower and discovering ley-line convergence points across the rewritten world, indicating travel beyond isolated Spire and knowledge of realm-wide magical changes.

status_history.note: The apprentice discovers hidden texts revealing the Ascension was not the first such working; previous civilizations performed similar rituals that left voids collapsing inward.
status_history.evidence: Ancient texts now readable under new starlight describe the Ascension in detail and suggest previous similar rituals consumed everything; Master deliberately obscured this knowledge during the apprentice's education.

status_history.note: Descends into ancient tower beneath Scorched Wastes and discovers a pre-human consciousness imprisoned in a weakening binding circle.
status_history.evidence: Young sorcerer finds ley-lines converge at tower's heart in a binding pattern; discovers entity 'older than demons' that remembers when stars fell differently and awaits the sky to break again.

status_history.note: The Young Sorcerer stands at the Obsidian Tower's sealing-stone, confronting the imprisoned entity whose song is breaking the seal from within. They have discovered a Codex Primordial fragment describing a possible Stellar Rite reversal requiring another cosmic sacrifice.
status_history.evidence: The young sorcerer discovers ley-line convergence is breaking the seal from inside; the imprisoned thing sings to be released. Archives contain fragments describing a reversal working that could undo Zareth's sacrifice but requires equal price: willing sacrifice of another cosmic entity.

characters.name: Malla
characters.bio: A young seer from Keth'moris who began experiencing fractured visions after the Crimson Convergence scrambled prophecy itself. She sketched obsessively and consulted with priests, scholars, and cartographers, but none could interpret the visions. Desperate to understand what is coming, she set out traveling to collect other fragments of vision from across the world.
characters.notes: Her visions mention a blank-faced archivist, a shadow-merchant, sea-folk rising from the deep, and stars that cast no shadows. Unresolved: what does she ultimately discover?

characters.name: Captain Merra
characters.bio: Captain Merra smuggles contraband and forbidden cargo across coastal waters, from plague-victims to stolen shadows. She is drawn into a deeper bargain when transporting a living oath-thing to the Sapphire Deep, gaining safe harbor but at the price of delivering further oath-things before the year's end. Her shadow has darkened, marking her as bound to the sea's memory.
characters.notes: Bound by a living contract to the deepkin; must find and transport one more oath-thing before year's end or face unknown consequences.

characters.name: Kael Stormbreaker
characters.bio: Kael Stormbreaker rules his mountain fastness with iron will and martial prowess, bending rival lords through strategy and strength for decades. When the new stars alter the world's power structure, a rival warlord launches an assault. Though his advantages diminish, Kael fights masterfully to allow his people to escape into the high passes before falling to the enemy.
characters.notes:
Kael dies watching the new stars, wondering what the cosmic shift means for the world he leaves behind. His final moments reflect genuine defiance rather than inevitable conquest.
Kael's death is consistent with canon status; new detail reveals the fastness is bound by generational blood-oath and will haunt whoever holds his seat. His strategic legacy is subtler than conquest.

status_history.note: Kael dies in combat to challenger Thorne, but his true victory is binding the mountain fastness itself to his bloodline through blood-oath magic, cursing his successor with a prison of living architecture.
status_history.evidence: Kael's blood-oath magic transfers to the victor; the fastness itself resists Thorne's command, and every stone, corridor, and torch-flame obeys only those of Kael's bloodright. The old warlord's final joke outlives him both.

characters.name: Isenna
characters.bio: Isenna is a seer whose life has been defined by visions—fragmentary glimpses of possible futures that more often mislead than clarify. Since the Conjunction, the nature of prophecy itself has changed, granting her true foresight. She reads the palm of a merchant seeking trade advice and sees caravans vanishing into the Scorched Reach and merchants transformed into neither living nor dead.
characters.notes: Has begun receiving true prophecies following the Conjunction event. Warns of something awakening in the bleached lands. May hold crucial information about the Stellar Rite's consequences.

characters.name: Elder Mage
characters.bio: The Elder Mage is a now-deceased master of sorcery who left behind the Codex containing knowledge of the Stellar Rite. They documented the terrible price the ritual would exact: the performer would be stripped of memory, joy, self, and everything that makes them human.
characters.notes: Dead before the story begins. Their Codex holds crucial lore about the Stellar Rite and its cost. The Young Sorcerer is their heir.

characters.name: Sylas the Deepwarden
characters.bio: Sylas is an ancient deepkin warden who dwells in the Cerulean Trench, the deepest place in the known oceans. Since the Violet Conjunction, Sylas has felt cosmic disturbance like a wound in the water itself. She gathers a council of elder deepkin and sends scouts to investigate strange currents and humming in the deep.
characters.notes: Suspects that cosmic magic has opened pathways long closed between deep and surface. Scouts report a humming as though the deep is learning to sing a new song. Awaiting understanding of what the new stars have brought.

characters.name: Old Ranger
characters.bio: An experienced ranger passing through Blackthorn Holdfast on her way south. She warns Ashara that the new stars have kindled something ancient in the desert stones, and that the boundaries between the deep Wastes and civilized lands will begin to blur within a year or two.
characters.notes: The source of the warning about the Wastes awakening. Her departure leaves Ashara to ponder alone what is stirring in the desert.

characters.name: Captain Vesta
characters.bio: Captain Vesta has built a smuggling empire trading in forbidden cargo—plague-victims, shadow-fragments, forbidden scrolls. She stands indebted to the abyssal singers of the Sapphire Deep, who demand binding-tokens to prevent her drowning. With nine months remaining, she seeks to orchestrate betrayals and broken oaths to produce the tokens required.
characters.notes: Her ship is her base of operations. The abyssal ledger always balances; her fate depends on producing binding-tokens before year's end.

characters.name: Kieran
characters.bio: A merchant in Pelimor who swore a partnership oath with his brother before witnesses. The brother has since vanished into the Ashen Wastes, leaving Kieran drowning in debt. Vesta sees him as a means to create a binding-token through orchestrated betrayal or reunion.
characters.notes: His broken oath or severed partnership could produce a binding-token for Vesta's abyssal masters.

characters.name: Vex the Fragment-Dealer
characters.bio: Vex discovers that her fragmented memories follow a pattern like a map, each gap corresponding to a location, name, or unpaid debt. Seven years ago she made a bargain in darkness to escape something worse. A Stellar Entity born from Zareth's sacrificed memories now hunts her, drawn to the magnitude of her curse, revealing that the working that stripped Zareth freed something already bound within her.
characters.notes: She must choose between reclaiming a memory that will destroy her sanity or letting the Stellar Entity starve in her fractured mind. The nature of her original bargain remains unclear.

characters.name: hedge-mage
characters.bio: A young mage arrives at Thornholt claiming to know how to seal the cosmic wound opened by Zareth's sacrifice. Ashara must decide whether to trust him, knowing his method will certainly require more sacrifice.
characters.notes: His arrival and claims are unverified. His method, if real, demands a price.

characters.name: The Young Hedge-Mage
characters.bio: A young hedge-mage discovers that the Grimoire of the Ancients has begun to rewrite itself after the Celestial Binding. When attempting a simple cantrip, it spirals into something vast and uncontrollable. They realize all sorcerers now walk a razor's edge as magic responds differently to the new starlight, and must decide whether to hide this knowledge or risk spreading it.
affiliations: Hermit Woman of the Ashen Wastes
