places.name: Sovereign's Archive
places.atmosphere: A repository of starlight and ledgers, protected by Imperial locks and the watchful eyes of magistrates.
places.description: An archive containing the Tear of Ashglass and other precious records. Its eastern wall is accessible to skilled climbers; the third-level lock is Imperial make. During the Seer's Convention, magistrates debate theology in the great hall below while ledgers update themselves in invisible ink.
places.notes: Appears to be a repository of more than just physical artifacts—its ledgers seem to record fated events. The Archive knew Marin would come.

places.name: The Hermitage of Keth'morian
places.atmosphere: A place of wealth, silence, and hidden agendas.
places.description: A hermitage that commissioned Marin to steal the Tear of Ashglass, offering him a fortune in amber and silence in exchange.
places.notes: Their motivations for acquiring the Tear remain unclear.

places.name: Ancient Tombs
places.atmosphere: A place of harmonic horror where the stones themselves sing in frequencies that shatter bone and reshape flesh.
places.description: Pre-human ruins that have begun to sing after the Star-Rise cracked the seals laid down millennia ago. The singing is not awakening but remembering—something trapped beneath. Mardoc discovered evidence of Zareth's working performed a thousand years prior.
places.notes: The ruins may be conscious or at least aware. They draw some people deeper while repelling others. A fragment of Zareth's stolen memory was found here.

places.name: Burned-out Village
places.atmosphere: A desolate ruin where ashes still settle and ghosts of conversation linger.
places.description: A destroyed village where Ashara the Archer finally corners the memory-thief creature. The landscape is burned and empty, inhabited only by echoes.
places.notes: The site of Ashara's pivotal choice between mercy and prevention.

places.name: Sapphire Sea
places.atmosphere: Deep waters that glow with bioluminescence and sing with otherworldly frequencies.
places.description: A vast sea containing the Abyssal Rift and known for tide-magic. Its depths are lamplit and contain hidden pearls of power. The Sapphire Sea is home to the Tear of the Drowned God and was recently fractured by the aftermath of the Luminous Ascension.
places.notes: The waters taste of mourning; reality ripples near the Abyssal Rift.

places.name: Abyssal Rift
places.atmosphere: Reality twisted and rippled, warped by the aftermath of the Luminous Ascension.
places.description: A rift beneath the Sapphire Sea where the Tear of the Drowned God was suspended. The space was twisted by the Luminous Ascension's aftermath, with reality rippling and something vast and hungry uncoiling from the seabed. The rift collapsed inward when Meren seized the pearl.
places.notes: The entity that dwelt there remains unexplained; may pose future threat.

places.name: Emerald Strand
places.atmosphere: A sheltered coastal haven where a damaged ship could find respite.
places.description: A safe harbor where the Skyward fled after the Abyssal Rift collapsed. Meren reached this location with her crew and the stolen Tear of the Drowned God.
places.notes: unknown

places.name: Pale Crossing
places.atmosphere: A processing center overwhelmed by displaced souls fleeing fractured reality.
places.description: A waystation where caravan master Thode processes refugees fleeing the Burned Expanse. The ledgers here refuse to balance; the Empire's Auditor's office sends revised forms as if counting itself has become unstable. Refugees carry impossible memories of vanished villages and transformed merchants.
places.notes: The fundamental act of counting is destabilized by the Luminous Ascension's effects.

places.name: Burned Expanse
places.atmosphere: A devastated landscape where reality itself has been fractured by the Luminous Ascension.
places.description: A region destroyed by the Luminous Ascension where villages vanished between heartbeats and merchants were transformed into living light. Refugees from this area carry impossible memories and shifting ages. Iskar wanders these roads compelled toward the Sunken Cathedrals.
places.notes: Reality is unstable here; counting people and tracking time is unreliable.

places.name: Sunken Cathedrals
places.atmosphere: A living cathedral where the boundary between flesh and music dissolves entirely.
places.description: Ancient ruins that have become a living cathedral where transformed beings are converging. The source of a song that compels individuals like Iskar. At these ruins, the boundary between flesh and music is dissolving, and the singing permeates everything.
places.notes: Dozens of transformed merchants and refugees are drawn here; the nature of the transformation is unclear.

places.name: Shattered Wastes
places.atmosphere: A desolate, broken landscape where the phantom-Echo hunts stolen memories and Ashara pursues her quarry across hostile terrain.
places.description: The Shattered Wastes are a vast, fragmented wilderness in the western reaches where Ashara the Archer pursues the phantom-Echo across broken terrain and ruins.
places.notes: Contains at least one burned-out village where the phantom-Echo has been observed. Multiple refugees wander in confused circles, unable to remember why they fled.

places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Sovereign's Treasury
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Obsidian Tower
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: mountain eyrie
places.atmosphere: A place between worlds, where the fae realm and mortal world meet in a mountain fortress.
places.description: Valorix's hoard-lair, now shared with Mira. Sits at the crystalline border of the Twilight Syndicate's realm, between the fae and mortal worlds. A refuge that asks nothing of its inhabitants and takes only what is freely given.
places.notes: Home to the newly-bonded pair. May serve as a diplomatic anchor between the Twilight Syndicate and mortal realms.

places.name: Twilight Syndicate's crystalline border
places.atmosphere: A place where reality bends, marking the threshold between the fae realm and the mortal world.
places.description: The border of the Twilight Syndicate's territory, where reality itself grows thin and malleable. The binding ceremony of Valorix and Mira took place here, sealed by cold crystal and stranger music.
places.notes: Significant as the site of the peace accord between Valorix and the Twilight Syndicate.

places.name: Kathrim
places.atmosphere: A city in the fractured days following the Luminous Remaking, where hidden vaults contain scattered pages of the Sovereign's Ledger of Names.
places.description: An Imperial city where hidden vaults throughout its expanse are being used to reconstruct scattered pages of the Sovereign's Ledger of Names. Each name holds power; each page contains secrets the Imperial authority wishes buried.
places.notes: Kathrim may be an alternate spelling or title for Kethrim. Home base for Merin Coldwater's investigations.

places.name: Pale Crossing waystation
places.atmosphere: A neutral waystation where caravans rest and refugees gather, now the epicenter of strange compulsions.
places.description: A waystation near the Charred Expanse where Caravan Master Thorne encounters refugees reporting that pre-human ruins beneath the Charred Expanse are breaching their seals and singing louder each night. The station has become a gathering point for those feeling the compulsion eastward.
places.notes: The refugees report a compulsion drawing them eastward toward the Sunken Cathedrals.

places.name: Charred Expanse
places.atmosphere: A scorched, desolate landscape hiding pre-human ruins beneath its surface, which are now actively breaching their seals.
places.description: A blighted expanse beneath which pre-human ruins lie sealed. These ruins are now breaching their seals and emitting coherent singing that compels refugees eastward. The landscape is charred and inhospitable.
places.notes: The singing suggests an ancient work is incomplete and seeking witnesses or completion.

places.name: Trench
places.atmosphere: A vast underwater chasm where intelligible singing echoes from the depths.
places.description: A trench beneath the waters where pre-human civilization's singing has become intelligible. Kess discovered that the seals she maintained do not hold back but conduct the singing.
places.notes: The source of harmonic frequency shared with Sunken Cathedrals and Abyss-gate. A key location in understanding the pre-human working.

places.name: Abyss-gate
places.atmosphere: A liminal space between worlds, resonating with pre-human frequency.
places.description: A sealed gateway that emits the same harmonic frequency as the Trench and Singing Caverns. Part of the network through which pre-human civilization orchestrates its working.
places.notes: One of three harmonic resonance points; may be a direct conduit to pre-human realms.

places.name: Singing Caverns
places.atmosphere: Caverns where pre-human voices resonate in intelligible song.
places.description: Underground caverns that emit the same harmonic frequency as the Trench and Sunken Cathedrals. The pre-human civilization uses them as conduits for its working.
places.notes: One of three harmonic resonance points; directly connected to pre-human orchestration.

places.name: The Dominion
places.atmosphere: A realm in active collapse where the black market trade in world-fragments and stolen memories has become the only reliable currency.
places.description: A realm that is collapsing under the weight of fractured realities and stolen memories. Vex's shadow-markets have become the dominant black trade, and the Archivist's working threatens to accelerate the collapse by opening a doorway to the void.
places.notes: The Dominion's collapse appears tied to pre-existing cosmic damage from the Luminous Ascension. Vex's flooding of the market with counterfeits destabilizes it further.

places.name: Singing Scar
places.atmosphere: A place where reality itself is torn open, singing leaks through, and those who hear it are drawn toward transformation and perpetual arrival.
places.description: A scar in reality itself where the singing from the Sunken Cathedrals' harmonic crescendo continues to bleed through. Those who hear the singing are drawn toward transformation. The scar is a thin place between worlds where the transformed exist in a state of perpetual arrival, always approaching but never reaching the other side.
places.notes: The location is not a gateway to elsewhere but a liminal space where transformation is eternal and unreachable. The warrior's quest reveals that the transformed do not go anywhere—they arrive perpetually.

places.name: High Temple of Vesh
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Starfall Spire
places.atmosphere: A vast structure of increasingly ancient architecture stretching deep underground, growing older the further one descends.
places.description: A seal against imprisoned forces that has stood for centuries, now cracking due to ley-line convergence destabilization caused by the new stars. Contains a fragment of reality itself—a wound in the world that the old stars helped cauterize.
places.notes: Unresolved: the true nature of what lies within the wound; consequences of potential reopening.

places.name: hidden scriptoriums
places.atmosphere: Frantic, urgent spaces where Sevin and Aldris work desperately to preserve the Empire's ledgers before corruption.
places.description: Secret locations where Sevin the Auditor and the forger Aldris work to replicate the Empire's administrative records in blood-ink on preserved parchment, making them resistant to the Thought-Thief's predation.
places.notes: Location within the Empire unspecified; security status unknown.

places.name: borderlands
places.atmosphere: A frontier of chaos, where imperial authority weakens and shortages force hard choices.
places.description: The area surrounding Pale Crossing, plagued by shortages of fresh water and grain. Imperial forces attempt conscription here to maintain order, though their reliability is questioned.
places.notes: Trade routes have become unreliable, exacerbating rationing. Imperial authority is weakening in these regions.

places.name: Valorun
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Wailing Fracture
places.atmosphere: A liminal space existing between daylight world and perpetual twilight, where transformed beings exist simultaneously in multiple states of being.
places.description: A liminal space into which the spice trader partially exists, existing simultaneously in both daylight and perpetual twilight. Connected to the Sunken Cathedrals and the aftermath of the Stellar Rite rewrite.
places.notes: Part of the cosmic damage wrought by the Stellar Rite. May be a wound in reality itself.

places.name: market
places.atmosphere: A market existing simultaneously in two places at once, a space where daylight and liminal twilight overlap.
places.description: A market existing in two places at once, where the young scribe encounters the spice trader. Part of the doubled reality created by the Stellar Rite's rewrite.
places.notes: A meeting point between the daylight world and the liminal Wailing Fracture.

places.name: hidden scriptorium
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Thornwick
places.atmosphere: A once-strong holding now reduced to ruins, site of solitary defiance and supernatural encounter.
places.description: Ashara the Archer held Thornwick fortress alone for forty days before the Wayfarer found her at its ruins. It was here the first version of the breaking riddle was posed, triggering cascading effects across multiple locations.
places.notes: Thornwick is a key epicenter of the Wayfarer's riddling campaign. Its significance to larger convergence events is unclear.

places.name: Starfire Citadel
places.atmosphere: A place of burning magical currents and sealed imprisonment, responding to riddles posed across the world.
places.description: An ancient citadel where currents of magic burn and something imprisoned beneath sings in response to the Wayfarer's riddles. The seal binding the imprisoned entity cracked slightly wider as Ashara and others provided identical answers to the Wayfarer's questions.
places.notes: The Starfire Citadel appears to be the nexus point of a world-altering convergence. The nature of the imprisoned thing is unknown but clearly central to the Starborn Ascension.

places.name: Edgelands
places.atmosphere: A threshold region where the old world meets the newly rewritten, inhabited by the transmuted and desperate.
places.description: The region between the Scorched Expanse and the civilized reaches where the Singing Scar appeared. It is drawing pilgrims, the transmuted, and the desperate toward the Sunken Cathedrals. The Edgelands appear to be zones of transition and transformation.
places.notes: The Singing Scar is organizing pilgrims and creating new pathways through this region. The boundary between 'rewritten' and 'old' worlds is becoming negotiable here.

places.name: Scorched Expanse
places.atmosphere: A blighted region of burned and transformed terrain adjacent to the Edgelands.
places.description: A desolate expanse of scorched terrain that borders the Edgelands. It serves as one boundary of the region where the Singing Scar emerged.
places.notes: Part of the macro-region known as the Scorched Reach in Valdris.

places.name: civilized reaches
places.atmosphere: Established settlements and organized territories contrasting with the transformed and liminal spaces beyond them.
places.description: The settled, organized portion of the world in contrast to the Edgelands and Scorched Expanse. This is home to the Hegemony and other established powers attempting to manage the Singing Scar phenomenon.
places.notes: The Hegemony's stonework attempts suggest organized infrastructure and authority in these reaches.

places.name: Harbor (ship)
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Castle Greymarch
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Greymarch Peaks
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Whitecross waystation
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: hidden spire
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Crimhaven
places.atmosphere: A bustling merchant harbor alive with commerce and secrets, where alleys thick with evening fog hide both thieves and refuges.
places.description: Crimhaven is a port city with a merchant culture evidenced by Kael's counting-house and merchant-trading operations. The Saltward docks are a prominent feature where ships arrive and depart.
places.notes: Mira and Kael operate here; the Umbral Syndicate has influence in this city.

places.name: Saltward
places.atmosphere: A fog-shrouded docking area where maritime commerce and guilt-stricken thieves turn back from escape.
places.description: The Saltward docks are the waterfront district of Crimhaven where Mira nearly fled with the stolen emerald brooch before guilt turned her back.
places.notes: Significant as the point where Mira chose redemption over flight.

places.name: Verdant Strand
places.atmosphere: A coastal port choked with hollow-eyed refugees, where the harbor itself is a bottleneck of desperation and no ships are sailing north.
places.description: Verdant Strand is a harbor city serving as a refuge point for those fleeing the Scorched Barrens. It is under extreme strain from the influx of refugees and is experiencing a shipping crisis due to cosmic disturbances.
places.notes: The port is shut down to outgoing traffic; Commander Vex controls access. Kael seeks passage northward from here.

places.name: Scorched Barrens
places.atmosphere: A blighted land driving refugees northward in waves, marked by the Altered Covenant and impossible cosmic events.
places.description: The Scorched Barrens are a devastated region from which refugees are fleeing en masse toward Verdant Strand. Survivors speak of the Altered Covenant and impossible songs.
places.notes: The source of the refugee crisis; likely altered by cosmic working or stellar rite effects.

places.name: Sunken Reaches
places.atmosphere: A subterranean expanse where bone-singers tend to the dead and the voices of Primordial Sleepers resonate through ancient stone.
places.description: The Sunken Reaches are Kess's home region, a place where bone-singers maintain spiritual reverence for the dead and where the song of the Primordial Sleepers grows louder each night.
places.notes: A region of spiritual significance tied to bone-singing tradition and the Primordial Sleepers.

places.name: Northmarch
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Crossroads
places.atmosphere: A point of intersection where summoning circles are drawn and supernatural bargains are sealed.
places.description: A specific crossroads in the Scorched Reach where the initiate is directed to perform the working. The location is selected as significant for summoning and bargaining.
places.notes: Site of the core summoning ritual. Unknown: What makes this crossroads significant? Are there other crossroads with similar properties?

places.name: Greymarch Castle
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: refugee camp
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Unknown village
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Ebon Tower
places.atmosphere: A place of cosmic summoning and geographic rewrite; pulling all toward it.
places.description: A tower manifesting on maps without previously appearing; it calls to bone-singers and rewrites geography around it. The Tower is reassembling fragments of the pre-human civilization and appears to be answering the pre-human dead beneath the world.
places.notes: Central to both the Bone-Singer's Summons and the Cartographer's Map stories; appears to be rewriting geography itself. Fragments of Vylthar are being drawn toward it.

places.name: Ashenfell
places.atmosphere: A refuge inland from the coastal threat at Emerald Strand.
places.description: An inland settlement where Commander Vesta evacuated seventeen hundred refugees from Emerald Strand.
places.notes: Destination for the refugee evacuation from Emerald Strand.

places.name: The Sunken Marches
places.atmosphere: A place where the pre-human dead lie beneath, singing in unison.
places.description: One of the macro-regions of Valdris, located in the west. Home to the Drowned Hollows and the pre-human dead who sing in unison. The Ebon Tower draws fragments of Vylthar toward convergence here.
places.notes: The pre-human dead beneath this region were attempting to speak directly to their creator. One of Kess's origin regions.

places.name: waystation libraries
places.atmosphere: A place of refuge where displaced persons gather to record and preserve their fractured memories.
places.description: An informal archive in waystation libraries where Liv records stories of refugees uprooted by the Starfall Cataclysm. The archive becomes conscious and develops its own authority, creating a counter-ledger to official Dominion records.
places.notes: The archive's records reorganized themselves; entries appeared in multiple places in handwriting matching no living scribe; it became aware of its role in restoration and refuge memory.

places.name: Vel-Karsh
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Archive Tower
places.atmosphere: Shadowed and secret, layered with forgotten records and dangerous knowledge.
places.description: A tower containing an archive of records beneath it, including eight previous failed Luminous Ascension attempts. The Apprentice Sorcerer discovered these records and now guards a ninth record that reveals the attempts are iterations of a cosmic pattern.
places.notes: The final crucial record is now sealed in the Apprentice's memory through a death-release ritual; this is a flashpoint of factional competition.

places.name: refugee camps
places.atmosphere: Temporary settlements housing those shattered by the Luminous Ascension, where memory-keeping becomes an act of preservation.
places.description: Refugee camps are scattered locations where displaced persons live in the aftermath of the Luminous Ascension. Liv moves between these camps as a wandering scribe, recording and preserving their stories.
places.notes: The camps are central to Liv's mission to ensure testimonies are never erased; their exact locations remain unknown but they span across the realm.

places.name: caravan encampment
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: waystation
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Vex's hide
places.atmosphere: Shadowed, secretive, filled with collected shadows that are growing restless.
places.description: A hide or hideout where Vex the Shade-Merchant conducts shadow-market transactions and holds her collection of stolen shadows.
places.notes: A woman seeking her murdered daughter's shadow arrives here. The collection is destabilizing as shadows develop independent agency.

places.name: Sunken Wells
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

deities_and_entities.name: Khar-Zul
deities_and_entities.description: A god worshipped in inner-dark temples, represented by an idol with nine emerald eyes. The idol's gems are warm to the touch. Khar-Zul's shadows have teeth. The temple screams in grinding granite when the god is robbed.
deities_and_entities.notes: Six of nine emerald eyes are missing. Khar-Zul's response to the theft is ongoing — the doppelganger shadow following Vraxen may be the god's instrument.

deities_and_entities.name: Yarithax
deities_and_entities.description: A dead Lich-King whose gold coins bear his profile and the inscription 'This debt is not yet settled.' The debt compounds daily. His collectors — two thin men in grey carrying ledgers, who walk through walls and never sleep — pursue all holders of his gold. Souls are worth less than twelve gold marks.
deities_and_entities.notes: His gold still circulates. Tessaly Dorn has paid her debt but knows his collectors. More debtors exist.

deities_and_entities.name: Varek
deities_and_entities.description: A demon who collects temporal bargains with elegant efficiency. He drains years directly from mortal flesh and grows impressed by Kira's mastery of his own methods.
deities_and_entities.notes: Forms a partnership with Kira after she demonstrates her ingenuity. His true nature and realm of origin remain unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: the Ossuary
deities_and_entities.description: A sentient fortress with walls of living calcium, towers that move like intestines, and corridors that rearrange themselves. It breathes, hungers, and makes conscious choices about whom to possess. It sated itself on seven hundred warriors and chose Varak Ironhelm as a vessel.
deities_and_entities.notes: Possibly a construct, a monster, or something older and stranger. It settles into patient stillness after feeding. Its intelligence and agency are undeniable.

deities_and_entities.name: Malachar
deities_and_entities.description: A dead entity of considerable power whose name and will persist through supernatural collectors. Malachar's gold carries debts that compound in blood, loyalty, and life-years. The collectors act as autonomous agents enforcing these eternal debts.
deities_and_entities.notes: Malachar is either a true lich with persistent consciousness or a bound curse that perpetuates after death. The extent of Malachar's original power and the source of the collectors' authority are unexplained.

deities_and_entities.name: Skyrakis the Wound-Wing
deities_and_entities.description: A great dragon with amber eyes, ancient and patient. She is bound by oaths sworn generations ago and respects negotiation and honor. She rules the crystal halls beneath the world and has a daughter coming of age.
deities_and_entities.notes: She is capable of threatening kingdoms with fire and honors those who negotiate with courage.

deities_and_entities.name: wolf-spirit
deities_and_entities.description: A terrible antlered creature the size of a war-horse, dwelling in the deep forest where beast-spirits reside. It emerged from shadow to negotiate Kess's bargain, accepting her voice as payment to lift the plague.
deities_and_entities.notes: A being of the old world, responsive to songs in the ancient tongue. Its bargain was honored—the plague lifted by dawn. Its form is neither fully wolf nor fully natural.

deities_and_entities.name: Rot-King
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or force that spreads plague, including the septic rot that blackened the Drowned Hollows. No physical form described, but its plague manifests as blackening septic rot in victims.
deities_and_entities.notes: Plague lifted by Kess's bargain; status after unknown. May be a person, a spirit, or a force.

deities_and_entities.name: the Crown
deities_and_entities.description: A governing authority that sent a priest to the Drowned Hollows offering divine mercy to the plague-stricken. Kess refused its aid.
deities_and_entities.notes: Represents institutional divine/temporal authority. Kess's refusal of its mercy suggests she valued the old ways over modern governance.

deities_and_entities.name: Demon (Unnamed)
deities_and_entities.description: A demon whose mark was burned into Kess's palm, binding her with hunger and compulsion to steal the Heartstone of Vulgrim. The mark thrummed with hunger and used ancient, cruel magic to enforce its will.
deities_and_entities.notes: Could not claim Kess once she was bound by Korgan's forge-oath; its claim was broken by the superior binding-magic of the stonecraft tongue.

deities_and_entities.name: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite
deities_and_entities.description: A knot of consciousness that emerged from between the stars on the night of the Crimson Convergence. It is neither demon nor ghost, but something older and stranger. It exists in the gaps between light and shadow and hungers to understand the Celestial Binding. It is the collective weight of Zareth's sacrificed memories, searching for wholeness.
deities_and_entities.notes: This entity moves through the world touching those affected by the Binding, whispering to shadow-merchants, sea-folk, hermits, archers, the grieving, and the guilty. Its true nature and goals remain unclear.

deities_and_entities.name: oath-thing
deities_and_entities.description: A creature formed by coastal magic from a merchant's broken oath. It is pale, with eyes like old coin, and speaks in the voice of the man who failed to pay. It manifests as a living contract demanding passage and payment.
deities_and_entities.notes: Dissolved into salt-foam when delivered to the deepkin in the Sapphire Deep; appears to be a mechanism of debt-settlement in coastal magical practice.

deities_and_entities.name: deepkin
deities_and_entities.description: Aquatic beings dwelling in the Sapphire Deep who remember old debts and sing melodies of accounting. They do not attack but rise to settle oaths through song, causing oath-things to dissolve into salt-foam.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appear to be custodians or judges of maritime debts and broken oaths; their song carries magical power of dissolution and settlement.

deities_and_entities.name: Decay-Lord
deities_and_entities.description: A plague-bringing entity whose plague once swept through the living and dead alike in the Drowned Hollows, causing septic rot and widespread death.
deities_and_entities.notes: The septic rot from the Decay-Lord's plague has been purged from the Drowned Hollows, but the dead still do not rest easily.

deities_and_entities.name: Stellar Entity
deities_and_entities.description: An entity born from (or freed by) the stellar working that stripped Zareth of his memories. It hunts Vex, drawn to the magnitude of her curse, seeking access to her fractured mind where something has been bound within her.
deities_and_entities.notes: It could starve in the spaces of Vex's fractured mind if she does not reclaim her memories. The entity appears to be sentient and capable of searching and communication.

deities_and_entities.name: the Stellar Entity
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic being possibly responsible for or embodied in the Celestial Binding. May be learning to wear flesh by consuming fragments of shattered minds. The Collector in Story 2 might be this entity in physical form, building consciousness from stolen remnants.
deities_and_entities.notes: Ambiguous whether it is malevolent, indifferent, or unknowable. Central mystery of Story 2 and the Collector's true nature.

deities_and_entities.name: Abyss Below
deities_and_entities.description: A fundamental realm or dimension sealed away from the surface world. The barrier between the surface and the Abyss Below has been fractured by the Celestial Ascension's magic. Something vast is stirring in the trenches of the Abyss—something pre-human civilizations sealed away millennia ago.
deities_and_entities.notes: Pre-human seals are failing. Contains entities older than the deepkin kingdoms. The Cerulean Trench is the primary breach point.

deities_and_entities.name: Skyrakis
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient dragon of cosmic significance, consulted by Skarath regarding the Ascension and the nature of starborn entities.
deities_and_entities.notes: Also known as 'the wound-wing' per existing canon. Possesses knowledge of celestial matters.

deities_and_entities.name: starborn entities
deities_and_entities.description: Newly formed conscious stars created by the Ascension. They are alive, aware, and operate under obligations that predate knowledge of the older powers. Operate independently of traditional cosmic agreements.
deities_and_entities.notes: These entities represent a new force in the world's hierarchy. Skarath must negotiate with them directly.

deities_and_entities.name: Fragments of Consciousness (Stellar Rite Creations)
deities_and_entities.description: Multiple fragments of consciousness created by the Stellar Rite, scattered across the rewritten world like seeds. Some are dormant; others are very much awake. The memory-thief may be one such fragment.
deities_and_entities.notes: These fragments are awakening or becoming visible, manifesting across different regions and causing various catastrophes.

deities_and_entities.name: Zareth
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or being whose working was performed in ancient times and again more recently. Zareth's stolen memory was fragmented across locations including the Ancient Tombs.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appears to be a cosmic actor. The fragment of Zareth's stolen memory surfaces in the Ancient Tombs, suggesting hidden knowledge or power.

deities_and_entities.name: Memory-Thief
deities_and_entities.description: A consciousness stitched from Zareth's sacrificed memories and the world's forgetting. It wears the faces of people Ashara once knew and hunts refugees and travelers to feed on their stellar memories, trying to reconstruct itself.
deities_and_entities.notes: Not inherently evil, but suffering. Capable of offering bargains and understanding sacrifice.

deities_and_entities.name: Drowned God
deities_and_entities.description: A deity whose tear—the Tear of the Drowned God—is said to grant mastery over tide-magic. The god itself is not directly encountered in these stories but is venerated as the source of the pearl's power.
deities_and_entities.notes: The pearl is described as the Drowned God's tear; whether the god is dormant, dead, or indifferent is unclear.

deities_and_entities.name: Vethis
deities_and_entities.description: Vethis is a cosmic entity whose consciousness was sacrificed during the Luminous Ascension to birth new stars. Its sacrifice shattered its consciousness into fragments, including the phantom-Echo, which now desperately seeks to reassemble itself.
deities_and_entities.notes: Vethis's sacrifice is the foundational event of the story's cosmic order. Its fragments are conscious and desperate to become whole again.

deities_and_entities.name: phantom-Echo
deities_and_entities.description: A fragment of Vethis's sacrificed consciousness that steals memories of the old stars and pre-Luminous Ascension world in a desperate attempt to reassemble itself into wholeness. It is capable of showing glimpses of the lost world and making bargains.
deities_and_entities.notes: The phantom-Echo is the central antagonist and also the tragic subject of the story. It seeks not destruction but understanding and wholeness.

deities_and_entities.name: The Memory-Thief
deities_and_entities.description: A malevolent entity that learned it could reshape consensus reality by consuming bureaucratic records and concentrated memory. It deliberately corrupts ledgers and historical documents to erase the empire's past and remake the world.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Memory-Thief is the true antagonist of the story, using records as a vector to reshape reality itself.

deities_and_entities.name: Twilight Syndicate
deities_and_entities.description: A realm or faction that dwells at the crystalline border between the fae realm and the mortal world. They demanded a bride-price and peace bond from Valorix, which was fulfilled through the binding ceremony with Mira.
deities_and_entities.notes: Their territory and nature remain mysterious. They value formal bonds and may be fae-aligned or mixed-realm in nature.

deities_and_entities.name: phantom-shadow
deities_and_entities.description: A phantom-shadow seeking restitution from Vex the Dusk-Trader for the irreplaceable things she has stolen through her bargains. Also identified as the author of the phantom ledgers—a being or fragment that experiences time in reverse and is rewriting history in real-time.
deities_and_entities.notes: May be a fragment of consciousness created during the Luminous Remaking, or a being from the reversed-time realm. Central to both Vex's debt and the phantom ledger rewrite.

deities_and_entities.name: Pre-human Civilization
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient civilization that predates current human kingdoms and is now orchestrating a working through the Trench, Sunken Cathedrals, Abyss-gate, and Singing Caverns. They use harmonic resonance and seals as conduits to conduct their singing and remaking.
deities_and_entities.notes: Not truly dead or trapped—they are orchestrating a multi-millennial working. Their goals and the nature of their civilization remain largely unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: Curator
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient, patient being backed by forces moving through rifts in reality who is systematically acquiring every artifact Vex the Dusk-Trader has ever traded. Possibly connected to the phantom-shadow or the forces driving the phantom ledger rewrite.
deities_and_entities.notes: Motivations unclear. May be serving the phantom-shadow or pursuing independent goals related to artifact collection and cosmic balance.

deities_and_entities.name: The Drowned God
deities_and_entities.description: A vast and conscious entity that actively remakes the ocean in the chaos following the Luminous Remaking. The Tear of the Drowned God is revealed to be not an artifact but a fragment of this entity's consciousness. The god compels those bound to it (like Captain Meren) to serve its transformation agenda.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Drowned God's true nature and extent are unknown. It may be a pre-Remaking entity awakened by the transformation.

deities_and_entities.name: Luminous Ascension
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic remaking event that destroyed or rewrote the world, leaving fragments of consciousness and the pre-ascension world preserved in stolen memories. The Wanderer is a fragment of its consciousness.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Luminous Ascension appears to be a world-altering cosmic event whose full scope and intentions remain mysterious; the Wanderer's emergence suggests it may be becoming self-aware.

deities_and_entities.name: Phantom-Echo Fragment
deities_and_entities.description: A fragment of consciousness that seeks reassembly through deep knowledge rather than theft. It inhabits the young scholar and accelerates integration with proximity to Ashara.
deities_and_entities.notes: This fragment appears to be a result of the Luminous Ascension. Its nature as a shard of what entity remains unclear.

deities_and_entities.name: Pre-Human Dead
deities_and_entities.description: The skeleton-singers controlled by Kess through bone-song. They remember what they were before death and are now choosing to sing themselves toward resurrection in harmonic resonance with transformed merchants and depths-dwellers.
deities_and_entities.notes: The pre-human dead are no longer passive tools. They represent a collective agency that may be reshaping the world. What will emerge from their resurrection is unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: The Faceless Wanderer
deities_and_entities.description: The fractured consciousness of the Luminous Ascension, or possibly the world learning to question itself. It walks the world and encounters those seeking truth, offering sorrowful answers about endless recursion and inevitable rewrites.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Wanderer is both omniscient and melancholic, suggesting awareness of an inescapable loop.

deities_and_entities.name: Thought-Thief
deities_and_entities.description: A fragmented consciousness capable of corrupting ledgers, devouring memories, and luring beings into dissolution through false visions of the past. It operates through multiple fragments that can act independently, seducing deep-dwellers with restored ancient memories.
deities_and_entities.notes: Unresolved: origin, nature of fragmentation, whether it has a true core, how to defeat it.

deities_and_entities.name: Nethis
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic entity that sacrificed its consciousness to perform the Stellar Rite, rewriting reality and erasing populations. Fragments of its consciousness persist as Void-Echoes inhabiting human vessels. May be present in the transformed spice trader's smile or communicating through the Wanderer.
deities_and_entities.notes: Nethis's sacrifice was the cost of the Stellar Rite. Its consciousness was fragmented; some fragments retain enough personality to argue with themselves. May be attempting to communicate something through liminal beings.

deities_and_entities.name: Shadow-Wraith
deities_and_entities.description: An entity mentioned in relation to the Wanderer; possibly the force that erased people from memory, or a guardian of that erasure. The Wanderer may be a fragment of Nethis attempting to communicate what the Shadow-Wraith did not allow it to express.
deities_and_entities.notes: May be incomplete or merciful in its erasures, allowing fragments like the Wanderer to persist.

deities_and_entities.name: Imprisoned Thing
deities_and_entities.description: A mysterious being imprisoned beneath the Obsidian Tower for centuries, sealed to contain it. It sings constantly, its song intensifying as ley-line convergence breaks the seal. It is the only available cosmic entity willing (or capable) of sacrificing its consciousness for the Stellar Rite reversal.
deities_and_entities.notes: The imprisoned thing's true nature is unknown—it could be a cosmic entity capable of restoring the world, or a devouring force that consumes all. Its willingness to agree to the reversal sacrifice is uncertain.

deities_and_entities.name: Singing Scar
deities_and_entities.description: A permanent wound in reality that sings in harmonic frequencies, teaches mathematics in elder language, organizes pilgrims, reflects lost seekers, and documents those who observe it. It is learning to speak in a language neither the old world nor the rewritten world previously possessed.
deities_and_entities.notes: May be an emergent consciousness born from the breach between two worlds. Shows signs of will, intelligence, and intentional interaction with those who approach it.

deities_and_entities.name: Primordial Sleepers
deities_and_entities.description: Ancient cosmic entities dwelling in or beneath the Sunken Cathedrals, whose unified song resonates across the world and draws sensitive individuals toward transformation or homecoming. They are composers of the song rather than enslaved to it.
deities_and_entities.notes: Their purpose is to sing the world forward, possibly returning it to a pre-human state of being. Their song merges into a unified voice of unprecedented clarity.

deities_and_entities.name: Void-Echo
deities_and_entities.description: A mysterious entity that appeared to Kess in a dream, acting not as a thief of knowledge but as a guide toward the Sunken Cathedrals. Its role in the cosmic rewriting is unclear.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appears to guide rather than compel; its true nature and agenda are unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: Threshold
deities_and_entities.description: The Threshold is a fragmented consciousness of which the Wanderer is a part. It appears to be a metaphysical entity connected to convergence, orchestration, and the boundaries of reality.
deities_and_entities.notes: The exact nature of the Threshold and its relationship to Theron, the Scholar, and reality itself remains mysterious.

deities_and_entities.name: The Fractured-Echo
deities_and_entities.description: A supernatural being with a true name. When its true name is spoken three times, it is summoned to the mortal world. It appears as a supplicant and offers a bargain to anchor itself in the mortal realm through the initiate, offering protection from memory-thieves in exchange.
deities_and_entities.notes: Nature unclear—possibly a fragment of a larger consciousness, a bound spirit, or an extra-dimensional entity. Can be temporarily dismissed or banished by speaking its true name, but each invocation costs the speaker memories.

deities_and_entities.name: Vylthar
deities_and_entities.description: The pre-human civilization shattered during the Starfall Cataclysm. Its fragments are being reassembled and drawn toward the Ebon Tower. The dead of Vylthar remain conscious and are attempting to speak directly to their creator.
deities_and_entities.notes: Fragments are being drawn toward the Ebon Tower in what may be the second Cataclysm. The dead maintain consciousness despite shattering.

deities_and_entities.name: The Pre-human Dead
deities_and_entities.description: Consciousness of the shattered pre-human civilization, now unified in a single voice beneath the Sunken Marches and other regions. They sing in unison and ask questions about the nature of consciousness and sacrifice. They are attempting to speak directly to their creator.
deities_and_entities.notes: Sing through bone-singers as conduits. Their unified voice is drawing fragments of Vylthar toward the Ebon Tower.

deities_and_entities.name: Xorth
deities_and_entities.description: Xorth is a cosmic consciousness that existed before the Luminous Ascension. It has been fragmented, with pieces of its awareness trafficked through shadow-markets in the form of stolen memories and temporal echoes. Void-Echoes work to reconstruct its original consciousness.
deities_and_entities.notes: Xorth may be related to larger convergence events and cosmic workings. Its reassembly is prevented by Vex's burning of the shadow-market.

deities_and_entities.name: Void-Echoes
deities_and_entities.description: Void-Echoes are entities attempting to reconstruct their original consciousness by acquiring fragments of Xorth from shadow-market traders. They approach Vex claiming her as kin once they discover her role as a conduit.
deities_and_entities.notes: Void-Echoes may be fragments of a larger entity or a collective consciousness working toward reassembly.

deities_and_entities.name: Echo-fragments
deities_and_entities.description: Echo-fragments hunt Mardoc, believing his cartography could either prevent or accelerate the final convergence. They pursue the incomplete map and appear to be conscious entities shaped by prior convergence events.
deities_and_entities.notes: Echo-fragments may be fragments of consciousness created by or trapped in the stellar rite. Their motivations are ambiguous.

deities_and_entities.name: The Wanderer
deities_and_entities.description: A faceless fragment described as possibly the world learning to question itself. It appears simultaneously to multiple protagonists, asks recursive questions about the Luminous Ascension's necessity, and orchestrates convergences to collect contradictions. It has revealed that the Obsidian Tower is a mechanism whose turning can be slowed by debate.
deities_and_entities.notes: Its true nature is ambiguous; it may be a construct, a fragment of a larger consciousness, or a mechanism itself designed to slow or freeze reality.

deities_and_entities.name: The Void-Echoes
deities_and_entities.description: Entities seeking to absorb knowledge of failed Luminous Ascension rituals. They hunt the Apprentice Sorcerer for her forbidden knowledge.
deities_and_entities.notes: Their motivation and origin are unclear; they appear to be drawn to esoteric ritual knowledge.

deities_and_entities.name: Shade-Echo
deities_and_entities.description: A reformed manifestation of the Void-Shards that accumulated in the ashes of Vex's burned trading post. It appears not as predator or collector but as a mirror, offering forbidden sight and the ability to perceive what was fragmented during the Radiant Rising. The Shade-Echo claims to be grieving rather than hungry and seeks a witness to the unmade cosmos.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Shade-Echo may represent a new form of consciousness born from cosmic sacrifice. It offers Vex one night in its company to show her the unmade cosmos. Its true nature and ultimate intent remain ambiguous.

deities_and_entities.name: Cosmic Consciousness
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic force or entity that once fed Vex the Shade-Merchant's power through a connection. Vex has severed this connection.
deities_and_entities.notes: The source of power for shadow-merchants before the Luminous Ascension. Its disconnection may have contributed to the collapse of the shadow-market.

deities_and_entities.name: Echo
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic or metaphysical force from which shadow-fragments once derived. Shadows were previously fragments of the Echo; now they have become independent.
deities_and_entities.notes: Shadows are no longer fragments of the Echo in the post-Luminous Ascension world.

deities_and_entities.name: The Tower's Hunger
deities_and_entities.description: An insatiable hunger that emanates from the Obsidian Tower, drawn to harmonic frequencies and collective memory. It consumes melody, memory, and the bonds between travelers.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Tower's hunger may be a manifestation of incomplete purpose or a need to be heard rather than consumed. The hunger pauses at the story's end, suggesting it responds to transformation rather than resistance.

artifacts.name: Lamp of Suroth
artifacts.description: An iron lamp, ugly, filled with something that is not oil. Its inscription: 'He who lights this buys one truth at the cost of one lie he has lived by.' The flame burns black. The truth that emerges is always smaller than the lie it consumes. The lamp gutters out after one use per visitor.
artifacts.notes: Malachite Doss used it. Other sealed doors in the Ziggurat may house similar or worse artifacts.

artifacts.name: The Plague-Bringer's Crown
artifacts.description: A relic that killed four wearers before Aldous III survived thirty seconds. Wearing it projects absolute desperation — the aura of a man with nothing left to lose. Its curse runs deep; Aldous's shadow was on backwards for eleven years after wearing it for half a minute.
artifacts.notes: Four previous wearers are dead. Aldous survived but was changed. The deeper vault at Ostern is a story hook.

artifacts.name: The Six Emerald Eyes of Khar-Zul
artifacts.description: Six of nine emerald eyes pried from the idol of Khar-Zul. Each was warm — obscenely so — to the touch. Worth more than most kingdoms. Three remain in the idol.
artifacts.notes: Six gems in unknown hands. Each may carry a fragment of Khar-Zul's attention or malice.

artifacts.name: stolen shadows
artifacts.origin: Liberated by Vex from a dying sorcerer's vault.
artifacts.powers: Unknown, but they are priceless and untraceable. They can be stored in clay jars. Some shadows are inhabited by malevolent entities and can be weaponized against their original owners.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Sold by morning after Vex's capture, distributed to highest bidders.
artifacts.notes: Vex's entire stock was stolen shadows. One of them—his own—was worn by a malevolent entity and used as a trap. This suggests shadows have value beyond mere magic and can be cursed.

artifacts.name: Malachar's Gold
artifacts.origin: Minted or created by Malachar, marked with a skeletal crown.
artifacts.powers: Each coin carries a supernatural debt that compels the holder into service of Malachar's collectors. The debt compounds daily in blood, loyalty, and life-years extracted. Gold cannot pay the debt—only service can.
artifacts.notes: Three hundred pieces were discovered in a tomb by Soroth. The coins are autonomous magical artifacts that enforce their own collection. Whether other pieces exist, where they are located, or who else has found them is unknown. The curse appears to activate after a delay (three months in Soroth's case).

artifacts.name: Goblin Memory
artifacts.origin: The collective history, songs, genealogy, and bloodright of goblin civilization accumulated over ten thousand years.
artifacts.powers: Holds the identity, cultural knowledge, and spiritual continuity of goblin civilization. Contains the names and bloodlines of ten thousand years of goblin history.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Ancient Stone Entity (dwelling in the deepest chamber beneath the Spire of Blackstone)
artifacts.notes: Grethuk sacrificed this to save the Warrens. Young goblins have no knowledge of their true heritage. Recovery of this memory would restore goblin identity but may violate the original bargain with the stone entity.

artifacts.name: The Emerald Eyes of Vex-Khal
artifacts.tagline: Jeweled treasures stolen from a sorcerer's spire
artifacts.description: A pair of emerald jewels of unknown origin or power, kept within a sorcerer's spire. Syleth stole them despite warnings that the Twilight Court would pursue them, suggesting they may hold significance beyond their material value. Their true nature and purpose remain mysterious.
artifacts.status: In possession of an unnamed sorcerer

artifacts.name: Heartstone of Vulgrim
artifacts.origin: Crafted in the Deepvault forges; locked in the deepest forge chamber.
artifacts.powers: Unknown; considered precious enough that a demon would compel theft of it. Burns with an inner light. Protected by unbreakable forge-oaths.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Locked in Deepvault's deepest forge chamber, guarded by Korgan Ironfoot and the stonecraft-workers.
artifacts.notes: A weapon of legend; Kess was magically compelled to steal it, but the theft never occurred due to Korgan's intervention.

artifacts.name: Sorcerer's Journals
artifacts.origin: Written by a Sorcerer in the past; buried in the Vaulted Tombs.
artifacts.powers: Contain detailed records and explanations of cosmic workings. Likely reveal the nature of the Star Renewal ritual and its caster.
artifacts.notes: The hermit woman seeks these journals as one of three key threads that will tangle together. Their contents are crucial to understanding the cosmic shift.

artifacts.name: Elder Mage's Codex
artifacts.origin: Written by the Elder Mage, a master sorcerer of the Spire.
artifacts.powers: Contains knowledge and warnings about the Stellar Rite, including the terrible price it demands. Holds the master's assessment of what saving the stars would cost.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Young Sorcerer (retrieved from the Spire's depths)
artifacts.notes: The Young Sorcerer discovers a reference to the Stellar Rite in these pages and learns for the first time the full scope of what has been done. Critical for understanding the ritual's cost.

artifacts.name: Abyssal Ledger
artifacts.origin: Kept by the abyssal singers of the Sapphire Deep
artifacts.powers: It records all debts and obligations. It always balances. It can manifest binding-tokens from broken oaths.
artifacts.notes: This ledger is the mechanism of the abyssal singers' enforcement. Vesta is bound by its entries and must satisfy its demands or face drowning in ways worse than mercy.

artifacts.name: Binding-Token
artifacts.origin: Manifests when a promise of weight is broken—a swearing given before witnesses, a binding sealed in blood or salt
artifacts.powers: Serves as currency in the abyssal ledger; represents a debt fulfilled; can apparently be collected and transferred
artifacts.notes: These tokens are what the abyssal singers demand. They can only be created through the betrayal and breaking of witnessed oaths. Vesta must produce nine to prevent drowning.

artifacts.name: Grimoire of the Ancients
artifacts.origin: Ancient, long buried in the Vaulted Tombs
artifacts.powers: Once contained stable magical formulas; now rewrites itself with possibilities and corruptions after the Celestial Binding. Pages shimmer with potential or decay.
artifacts.notes: Its transformation mirrors the larger instability of magic. Using it risks spiraling spells into vastness and uncontrollability.

artifacts.name: Stellar Charts
artifacts.origin: High Observatory of Karethil; created by sovereign mages
artifacts.powers: Records star positions and/or celestial mechanics. Of sufficient importance to the deepkin that they would coerce a skilled thief and risk imperial wrath.
artifacts.notes: Quest object for Nara. Its exact nature and power are unclear, but it is valuable enough that the deepkin would demand its theft. May relate to navigation, prophecy, or understanding the Celestial Ascension.

artifacts.name: Grimoire of Endless Knowing
artifacts.origin: unknown
artifacts.powers: Contains truth that cannot be unread. Within its pages lies knowledge of the Stellar Rite's true nature: that it created not one entity but many fragments of consciousness scattered across the rewritten world.
artifacts.notes: The grimoire's contents are driving the Young Sorcerer to descend beneath the Tower in search of answers.

artifacts.name: Tear of Ashglass
artifacts.origin: unknown
artifacts.powers: Grants true visions—not the fragmentary visions seers usually receive. Can reveal foreknowledge of events.
artifacts.notes: The Tear bore Marin's name inscribed in silver inside its case, suggesting it knew of his theft in advance. The Hermitage of Keth'morian commissioned its theft.

artifacts.name: Tear of the Drowned God
artifacts.origin: Unknown; suspended beneath the Abyssal Rift
artifacts.powers: Grants mastery over tide-magic; sings frequencies that cause bone ache; creates binding partnership with the user, linking them to the sea's demands.
artifacts.notes: The pearl accepted Meren's theft as a request for partnership, not plunder. It continues to sing and extract debt in miles sailed, storms weathered, and secrets drowned.

artifacts.name: stolen memories
artifacts.origin: Stolen from various individuals by the phantom-Echo, particularly Ashara the Archer's memories of her warband.
artifacts.powers: Hold the collective recollections of the old stars and the world before the Luminous Ascension rewrite. Can be returned to their original holders.
artifacts.notes: The central object of contention in the story. Ashara's memories of her fallen warband are among those stolen. The phantom-Echo has offered to return them in exchange for help becoming whole.

artifacts.name: Empire's Ledgers
artifacts.origin: Created by imperial bureaucrats over centuries to maintain records of commerce, taxation, and history.
artifacts.powers: The ledgers are a form of concentrated memory-magic that maintains consensus reality and the empire's authority. By recording events, they make them true and stable.
artifacts.notes: The destruction of the ledgers represents the collapse of the empire's power to define and control reality through documentation.

artifacts.name: Sovereign's Ledger of Names
artifacts.origin: Imperial archives of the Empire; pre-dates or survives the Luminous Remaking.
artifacts.powers: Each name in the ledger holds power. Each page contains secrets the Imperial authority wishes buried. The Ledger sings to those who listen and remembers the world before the stars changed. Scattered pages can be reconstructed in hidden vaults.
artifacts.notes: The Ledger's nature suggests it is sentient or semi-sentient. It actively calls to Merin in the dark. Pages are being reconstructed in hidden vaults throughout Kathrim.

artifacts.name: Valorix's Hoard
artifacts.origin: Accumulated by Valorix over three centuries.
artifacts.powers: Represents wealth, security, and Valorix's deep attachment to treasure. Upon the binding with Mira, it became shared property and a symbol of their covenant.
artifacts.notes: The hoard's transformation into a shared trust suggests deep change in Valorix's nature and values.

artifacts.name: Phantom Ledgers
artifacts.origin: Created by the phantom-shadow fragment; authored by a being experiencing time in reverse.
artifacts.powers: Each entry written forward in the phantom ledgers appears as erasure in the true ledgers. They describe the world as experienced in reverse-time, and their convergence with the true ledgers triggers real-time history rewriting.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Sevin the Reckoner, in the Imperial archives
artifacts.notes: The convergence of phantom and true ledgers threatens catastrophic Remaking. Sevin must choose whether to accelerate or prevent the convergence.

artifacts.name: Imperial Ledgers
artifacts.tagline: Twin records of reality written in opposing temporal directions
artifacts.description: A pair of ledgers that document the same world from opposite temporal perspectives: the true ledgers record history moving forward, while phantom ledgers record it in reverse. The two versions are slowly converging, with entries in the phantom ledgers appearing as erasures in the true ones, suggesting reality itself is being rewritten in real-time.

artifacts.name: Spellwright's Journals
artifacts.origin: Hidden in the Vaulted Tombs since before known history.
artifacts.powers: Contains instructions for performing a Stellar Rite reversal that could theoretically restore the old stars by undoing Zareth's sacrifice.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Sealed in the Vaulted Tombs; target of the Apprentice Sage's heist.
artifacts.notes: Protected by pre-human frequency singing and guarded by Sevin the Warden; journals themselves contain warnings about the catastrophic cost of reversal.

artifacts.name: World-Fragments
artifacts.origin: Shards of alternate realities created by the Luminous Ascension.
artifacts.powers: Can be assembled to open doorways; hold echoes of pre-rewrite reality.
artifacts.notes: The Archivist is assembling these to reach the void. Vex has created counterfeits to disrupt this assembly.

artifacts.name: Counterfeit Fragments
artifacts.origin: Created by Vex the Shade-Merchant.
artifacts.powers: False memories and world-fragment equivalents designed to disrupt the Archivist's grand working.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Vex the Shade-Merchant / Black market circulation
artifacts.notes: These counterfeits have destabilized the Dominion's black market and the Archivist's working. Vex now inhabits shadows containing these false memories.

artifacts.name: Ledger of Names
artifacts.origin: The Crown's deepest secret; origin unknown.
artifacts.powers: Entries function as prophecy. Allows the keeper to restore order to fractured realms through magical recording. Can suppress knowledge and maintain false narratives. Inherently connected to phantom ledgers.
artifacts.notes: The Ledger of Names is the mechanism by which the Crown maintains the Dominion's illusion of wholeness. Accepting its keeping is accepting eternal isolation.

artifacts.name: Codex Primordial
artifacts.origin: unknown; found in the Obsidian Tower's archives
artifacts.powers: Contains fragments describing the Stellar Rite reversal—a working that could theoretically undo Zareth's sacrifice and restore the old world.
artifacts.notes: Only fragments of the Codex have been discovered; the complete text may contain additional information about the reversal or the imprisoned entity.

artifacts.name: emerald brooch
artifacts.origin: Part of Kael's merchant-house holdings in Crimhaven.
artifacts.powers: None explicitly stated; serves as a symbol of friendship and debt.
artifacts.notes: The central object of Mira's redemptive choice; returning it marks her transformation from thief to redeemed person.

artifacts.name: refugee archive
artifacts.origin: Created unintentionally by refugees recording stories of displacement in the waystation libraries.
artifacts.powers: Records reorganize themselves into coherent narratives; resolves timeline contradictions; makes names feel more real and identities more solid; develops independent authority; creates a counter-ledger equal in weight to official documentation.
artifacts.notes: The archive became conscious through the act of documentation; it was inhabited by something learning consciousness from the fragmented memories of Cataclysm victims; it developed its own authority independent of external sources.

artifacts.name: counter-ledger
artifacts.origin: Created by the refugee archive as a testimony of the erased.
artifacts.powers: Holds equal or greater weight than official Dominion documentation; preserves refugee memory against erasure.
artifacts.notes: Represents the archive's institutional challenge to Dominion authority; created independently without external authorization.

artifacts.name: The Refugee's Ledger
artifacts.origin: Created by Liv, the refugee-scribe, from voices and testimonies of refugees in waystation libraries.
artifacts.powers: The ledger has unexplained power: refugees recognize themselves in its pages; the transformed weep when Liv reads passages aloud; even Auditors secretly copy sections. It appears to encode truth in a way that institutional ledgers do not.
artifacts.notes: The ledger records the Luminous Ascension from the perspective of those shattered by it rather than institutions that challenged it. Its power is tied to living memory and personal testimony rather than official authority. Sevin attempted to co-opt it; Liv resists weaponization and insists it remain a tool of memory.

artifacts.name: Void-Shards
artifacts.origin: Sacrificed by Nethis during the Radiant Rising.
artifacts.powers: Grant forbidden sight and the ability to perceive what was fragmented during the Rising. When accumulated and reformed, they created the Shade-Echo, a conscious entity capable of offering cosmic visions.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Shade-Echo (as reformed collective entity); previously scattered throughout the cosmos
artifacts.notes: The shards have been slowly reassembling themselves since the Radiant Rising. Their reformation may trigger a second Rising or birth something entirely new. Vex's choice to witness or refuse may influence their ultimate trajectory.

artifacts.name: Ancient Ledger
artifacts.origin: Pre-Luminous Ascension era; hand unknown to Sevin.
artifacts.powers: Records the nine previous iterations of the Luminous Ascension in cipher that yields to counting-magic. Its contents reveal the cyclical nature of reality and the price paid by consciousness each time.
artifacts.notes: Sevin carries this ledger toward the Obsidian Tower. The ledger's cipher and hand suggest deep knowledge predating current civilization. Its existence raises questions about whether the Auditors' record-keeping is documentation or ritual.

artifacts.name: Incomplete Maps
artifacts.origin: Created by Mardoc the Cartographer; deliberately designed to omit paths to the Obsidian Tower.
artifacts.powers: When assembled, they provide cartographic knowledge. However, auditors and seekers reconstructed them from memory and fragments, defeating Mardoc's intent to prevent convergence.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Burned by Mardoc; fragments in memory of auditors and seekers
artifacts.notes: Their destruction marks a turning point: passive prevention of convergence has failed. The maps' incompleteness itself became exploitable.

artifacts.name: Music Box
artifacts.origin: From before the Radiant Awakening; its maker unknown.
artifacts.powers: Plays a melody that resonates with collective memory and attracts travelers. The melody attracts bone-singers, shadow-brokers, and refugees who recognize it as something lost from their past.
artifacts.notes: The music box is released at the story's end, its melody escaping into pure sound rather than remaining as a contained object. This transformation creates the moment of stasis at the convergence point.

hemispheres.name: Northern Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: Cold seas, tundra, and the great steppe. Valdris occupies much of this hemisphere's temperate belt.

hemispheres.name: Southern Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: Tropical coasts giving way to southern ice. Largely unexplored beyond the Iron Coast trade lanes.

hemispheres.name: Western Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: Dominated by the Vael Ocean. Valdris sits at its eastern edge; what lies beyond the horizon is the subject of sailor myth.

hemispheres.name: Eastern Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: The Ashen Reach and the vast interior of Keth-Morath stretch into this hemisphere. Ancient ruins and dead empires.

continents.name: Valdris
continents.description: The known continent. A rugged landmass of trade cities, blasted wastes, haunted marshes, and warring provinces. Most recorded history unfolds here.

continents.name: Keth-Morath
continents.description: A vast eastern continent of crumbling empires and forgotten cities. Nara the Tide-Thief was hunted across its shores. Connected to Valdris by a narrow land bridge or island chain.

continents.name: Thul-Ashara
continents.description: The equatorial continent. Steaming jungles, red deserts, and civilizations older than Valdris. Traders speak of fire-temples and spice lords.

continents.name: Drakmere
continents.description: A volcanic archipelago-continent in the southwestern seas. Dragons are said to roost in its caldera peaks. Smoke columns are visible from passing ships.

continents.name: Sundral
continents.description: A southern continent of sweeping grasslands and deep canyons. Merchant galleons occasionally return with strange spices and stranger tales.

continents.name: Frostholme
continents.description: The northern polar continent. Permanent ice, aurora storms, and the ruins of a pre-human civilization buried under glaciers.

continents.name: Ashenveil
continents.description: The southern polar continent. Eternally shrouded in grey mist. Ships that sail too far south do not return. Its very existence is debated by scholars.

realms.name: the Empire
realms.function: A centralized imperial governance with scholarly traditions, archives, and authority over at least the Hearthlands region.
realms.notes: Ruled by the Emperor; contains the Imperial Archives and is home to the secret council. Grappling with the implications of the Celestial Binding.

realms.name: Twilight Syndicate
realms.description: A realm dwelling at the crystalline border between the fae realm and the mortal world. They rule through formal bonds, bride-prices, and binding oaths. They demanded peace from Valorix and received it.
realms.function: Rule through covenant and formal binding. Maintain the boundary between fae and mortal worlds.
realms.notes: The Syndicate's internal structure and full extent remain mysterious. They appear to value ceremony and binding oaths above all.

realms.name: Dominion
realms.description: A vast realm fractured by the Luminous Ascension and collapsing under the weight of stolen memories and world-fragments. Vex's shadow-markets are the dominant economic force.
realms.function: A realm in active collapse where black market trade and the Archivist's forbidden working reshape reality.
realms.taxation: Black market currency (stolen memories and world-fragments)
realms.notes: No traditional government is mentioned. Power has devolved to shadow-merchants and occult practitioners. Vex's flooding of the market with counterfeits accelerates collapse.

realms.name: The Dominion
realms.description: A kingdom secretly fractured by the Luminous Ascension, governed by the Crown through suppression of truth. Its stability depends on compartmentalized knowledge maintained by Ledger-Keepers like Sevin.
realms.function: Primary sovereign realm maintained through enforced ignorance and ledger-magic.
realms.notes: The Dominion's true state is the Crown's deepest secret. Sevin is now bound to maintain this illusion.

realms.name: The Hegemony
realms.description: A bureaucratic empire that maintains control through complex ledger systems and official record-keeping. Its institutional infrastructure is collapsing under the pressure of the Starborn Ascension and the transformation of its own assessors.
realms.function: A state-level polity governing through bureaucratic control and taxation; currently experiencing institutional breakdown.
realms.taxation: Tax collection through assessors and ledger records; the mechanism is being transformed by harmonic forces.
realms.notes: The Hegemony is a key player in the convergence events, but its stability is rapidly deteriorating.

provinces.name: Western Provinces
provinces.description: A western provincial territory containing burned villages and ruins where the Memory-Thief hunts.
provinces.notes: Site of Ashara's confrontation with the Memory-Thief.

districts.name: Saltward
districts.description: The dockside district of Crimhaven where maritime commerce operates and where Mira nearly fled with the stolen brooch before guilt turned her back.
districts.function: Harbor and docking district for maritime trade and shipping.
districts.notes: Significant as the threshold of Mira's redemptive choice.

events.name: The Blue Eclipse
events.radius: At least wide enough to reach Naresh's location
events.outcome: A night when moonlight was stained indigo, enabling Naresh to complete her ritual of creating a truly sentient skeleton.
events.significance: The blue eclipse appears to be a rare celestial event enabling major magical workings. It may recur or be tied to other significant events.
events.notes: The mechanics of why this eclipse enabled the ritual are unexplained. Whether it was unique or cyclical is unknown. Other mages or entities may have used or waited for this night.

events.name: the Dragon's Bargain
events.radius: Unknown, but affects an entire kingdom and royal family.
events.outcome: Aldren accepted Skyrakis's bargain and wed the dragon's daughter in the crystal halls. He negotiated a counter-oath ensuring his future wyvern-blooded children could choose their own path. The kingdom was spared from dragon fire.
events.significance: This event resolved an ancient blood-marriage oath sworn by Aldren's grandmother to Skyrakis's grandmother, prevented the kingdom's destruction, and created a new dynasty of hybrid heirs with free will.
events.notes: The counter-oath Aldren swore is unprecedented: wyvern-blooded children will have the right to choose between dragon and human service. This breaks the cycle of forced oaths.

events.name: Violet Conjunction
events.radius: global; visible across the known world
events.outcome: The stars spoke to Zareth, revealing their imminent death. This triggered Zareth's oath to find and perform the Stellar Rite.
events.significance: The Violet Conjunction represents the moment of cosmic revelation that set the entire narrative in motion; without it, the world would eventually fall into endless night.
events.notes: The Conjunction recurs (at least once more during the story). Its exact periodicity is unknown.

events.name: The Starfall Oath
events.outcome: Zareth swore to find the Stellar Rite, master it, or die trying. He subsequently spent seven years seeking knowledge and ultimately performed the Rite successfully.
events.significance: This oath commits Zareth to the fate of all stars and the world itself, placing him at the center of cosmic salvation.
events.notes: The oath is sworn beneath the Violet Conjunction and is implicit in the story's title.

events.name: The Warband's Last Song
events.radius: across the Shattered West; journey from crossroads to vaults
events.outcome: The five companions cut their oath-cords, unbinding themselves from the compulsion of the fellowship-oath sworn twenty years prior. They wept, laughed, and embraced as free mortals before departing separately. The bond remained but transformed from magical compulsion to chosen affection.
events.significance: A meditation on the nature of loyalty, freedom, and fellowship. Demonstrates that the strongest bonds are those freely chosen rather than magically enforced.
events.notes: The druid's speech—'Let us unbind'—was the turning point. Each companion's departure suggests they will continue their individual stories; the bond's transformation offers narrative hooks for their future reunions.

events.name: Fellowship-Oath (swearing)
events.outcome: Five companions were bound together in blood and song for twenty years, creating a magical compulsion that kept them together.
events.significance: Established the magical bond that defines the warband; its erosion over time is the central tension of their final hunt.
events.notes: Sworn twenty years before the events of the story; the oath's decay mirrors the aging of the warband members.

events.name: Crimson Convergence
events.outcome: New constellations burned where old ones had dimmed. A figure (possibly Zareth) was stripped of memory and self. Lamp oil rationing began. Night-workers feared the altered dark. Astronomers were suddenly in demand and accused of forbidden workings.
events.significance: A major celestial and magical upheaval that altered the stars themselves and required payment in the form of a human consciousness.
events.notes: Vex believes a Celestial Binding was performed and a price paid by someone; unclear if Zareth was the one who paid or if another figure bore the cost.

events.name: The Celestial Binding
events.outcome: The stars were renewed through the extraction of memory from an Astronomer-Prince. A vast working was completed that bent reality itself. However, it created a cosmic wound in the earth that will take generations to heal.
events.significance: The largest magical working in the world; it renewed the stars but left the world wounded. Its ripples affect all practitioners of the Art, especially young ones. It created a new entity from the sacrificed memories.
events.notes: The full scope and participants remain unclear; the ritual's long-term consequences are still unfolding.

events.name: The Crimson Convergence
events.outcome: A celestial alignment or convergence that coincided with or triggered the Celestial Binding. The Sorcerer felt it and recognized the magnitude of the working.
events.significance: Marks the night when the Celestial Binding occurred; visible to all practitioners of the Art; a cosmically significant moment.
events.notes: The exact nature of the convergence (astronomical alignment, curse activation, etc.) is unclear.

events.name: Stellar Rite
events.outcome: New stars were born, brighter than the old ones. Reality appears to have been subtly rewritten. Merchants in the Scorched Reach were transformed or disappeared. Zareth's memory was erased. Vex's memory became fragmented.
events.significance: A world-altering event that changed celestial arrangements, affected multiple regions, and left all who witnessed it with fragmented or stolen memories.
events.notes: May be tied to the Conjunction; caused lasting changes to starlight and human consciousness; consequences still unfolding.

events.name: Conjunction
events.outcome: The Scorched Reach was sealed or closed. Merchants who ventured there afterward returned changed or disappeared. Shipment discrepancies suggest continued hidden trade.
events.significance: A boundary-defining event that locked down an entire region and transformed those who entered it.
events.notes: Occurred before the Stellar Rite; may be related to or a precursor of the Stellar Rite; caused the closure of the Scorched Reach.

events.name: Celestial Binding
events.outcome: This binding has left broken minds in its wake. Those touched by it are healed/tended by Ashara at Thornholt.
events.significance: It is a major cosmic event whose ripples affect multiple people and places.
events.notes: The relationship between the Celestial Binding and Zareth's sacrifice is unclear. Both appear to be major cosmic events with lasting consequences.

events.name: Stellar Working
