events.outcome: The Stellar Working renewed the stars and altered the celestial arrangements of the world. What appeared to be a desperate improvisation was revealed to be the completion of a design laid down before kingdoms existed.
events.significance: The Stellar Working is the mechanism by which the Celestial Binding occurred. Its revelation as pre-planned rather than improvised suggests inhuman, cosmic forces have been orchestrating events across vast timescales.
events.notes: Central unanswered question: Was Zareth truly choosing, or merely the final piece of an ancient plan? The ruins' star-charts suggest the latter.

events.name: The Hermit's Census
events.radius: across the world; the Hermit has counted thousands
participants: Hermit Woman of the Ashen Wastes
events.outcome: The Hermit emerges from her isolation to reveal that the Celestial Binding has fractured the consciousness of thousands of ordinary people across the world. No one escaped its touch entirely.
events.significance: This discovery fundamentally reframes the Celestial Binding from a localized or targeted working into a world-spanning catastrophe affecting every human consciousness. The wound is not external but internal and may be irreparable.
events.notes: The Hermit's message suggests that healing or repair will require approaches beyond conventional magical or martial solutions. The true scope of the Binding's effects has been hidden until now.

events.name: Celestial Ascension
events.outcome: Reality was fundamentally altered. Maps rearrange themselves. Fragments become unstable. Pre-human ruins awaken and sing. A new constellation blazes in the sky, brighter and hotter than before. Merchants vanish. Memory is stolen or transformed.
events.significance: The Celestial Ascension is a world-altering event that has broken normal reality. It was supposed to be a solution or ending, but instead opened something far larger. It affects magic, memory, geography, and celestial mechanics.
events.notes: Core mystery of all three stories. Unresolved: what caused it? Was it intentional? What was it supposed to accomplish? Why did it fail or transform into something else?

events.name: The Ascension
events.radius: world-wide; affects the cosmic order itself
events.outcome: A new constellation was created with a new debt paid by unknown powers. The agreement between sky and earth was ruptured. Ancient oath-tracking abilities fracture. New stars prove to be conscious entities with their own obligations.
events.significance: Fundamentally alters the cosmic order and the hierarchies of power that governed the world. The old pacts between sky and earth are no longer sufficient.
events.notes: Requires renegotiation of all ancient pacts. Starborn entities are now conscious actors in the world's politics.

events.name: Seer's Convention
events.outcome: During the Convention, three head magistrates argued theology in the great hall, allowing Marin to steal the Tear of Ashglass without triggering alarms.
events.significance: The Convention's timing enabled a theft that had apparently been orchestrated long in advance.
events.notes: Unknown who organized Marin's involvement or why the Tear needed to be moved.

events.name: The Star-Rise
events.outcome: A reality-rewriting event that cracked the seals on the Ancient Tombs, caused villages to vanish overnight, transformed merchants into living light or erased them entirely, and made ledgers unstable. Refugees flee westward from the Scorched Reach.
events.significance: The Star-Rise appears to be a fundamental cosmological shift that has altered the rules of existence. It may have triggered the awakening of ancient workings.
events.notes: The Star-Rise may be related to Zareth's working. It has consequences that ripple across the mapped and unmapped boundaries of reality.

events.name: The Stellar Rite
events.outcome: The stars were fundamentally changed. Zareth's sacrificed memories were scattered across the world, birthing new entities. Old stellar memories were erased from consciousness.
events.significance: The foundational catastrophe reshaping all of Edhra. Every story is post-Rite.
events.notes: The Rite transformed all matter subtly, like ink soaking into parchment. Reality itself has been rewritten.

events.name: The Luminous Ascension
events.outcome: Reality fractured; villages vanished; merchants transformed into living light; the fundamental act of counting became unstable; abyssal rifts twisted and entities emerged.
events.significance: This cataclysm has shattered the Empire's administrative capacity and spawned widespread transformation. It is the root cause of refugee crises, dimensional instability, and the convergence at the Sunken Cathedrals.
events.notes: The exact nature and cause remain mysterious; aftermath continues to unfold.

events.name: The Memory-Thief's Bargain
events.tagline: Memories stolen. Compassion offered. Choice made.
events.radius: across the western reaches and Shattered Wastes
events.outcome: Ashara corners the phantom-Echo in the burned-out village and discovers its true nature. Rather than combat, the phantom offers a bargain: help it become whole, and it will return the stolen memories. Ashara is faced with a critical choice that will determine whether she recovers her warband's memory or risks a cosmic rewrite.
events.significance: This event represents a potential turning point in the cosmic order. The phantom-Echo's bid for wholeness could trigger another Luminous Ascension-scale rewrite, and Ashara's choice will determine the fate of countless memories and possibly the world itself.
events.notes: Unresolved: Will Ashara help the phantom-Echo become whole? Will she destroy it? What are the consequences either way? The event has not yet concluded.

events.name: Luminous Ascension
events.tagline: Stars reborn. Consciousness shattered. World rewritten.
events.outcome: Vethis sacrificed its consciousness to birth new stars, fundamentally rewriting the celestial order and erasing or altering memory of the old stars and pre-Ascension world. This sacrifice fragmented Vethis's consciousness into fragments like the phantom-Echo.
events.significance: The Luminous Ascension is the foundational cosmic event of this story's world. It created the new stars, erased the old, and fundamentally altered the nature of reality. The memory-theft driven by the phantom-Echo is an attempt to recover what was lost in this rewrite.
events.notes: This is a past event but with ongoing consequences. The phantom-Echo is a direct result of the Luminous Ascension and seeks to undo or understand its effects.

events.name: The Auditor's Ruin
events.radius: Across the entire empire as memory and records are systematically corrupted
events.outcome: The Imperial Archives are destroyed. The empire's past is largely erased. The old certainty built on ledgers and documentation collapses, forcing a reckoning with uncertain reality.
events.significance: This event reveals that bureaucratic records are a form of concentrated magic and that the empire itself is a spell maintained through documentation. It marks the end of the old empire's authority.
events.notes: Sevin's final choice—to restore the old lie or accept new uncertainty—will determine the empire's future trajectory.

events.name: Luminous Remaking
events.outcome: The stars and world were fundamentally rewritten. The Sovereign's Ledger of Names was scattered; Imperial authority fractured. Days following the remaking remain unstable.
events.significance: A cosmic working that changed the fundamental nature of reality itself. The Ledger now remembers the world before the stars changed.
events.notes: The exact nature and cause of the Luminous Remaking is unknown. Its consequences continue to unfold. May be connected to the pre-human work incomplete.

events.name: The Corsair's Reckoning
events.radius: across the sea and into the abyss
events.outcome: Meren discovers her debt to the Tear of the Drowned God is actually a transformation into something neither wholly mortal nor wholly divine. She is compelled to sail toward the Abyss-gate.
events.significance: This reveals that the Tear is a fragment of a conscious entity actively remaking the ocean. The transformation of Meren and her crew suggests the Drowned God is reshaping reality in its domain.
events.notes: The nature of Meren's transformation and whether it will be completed or reversed is unknown.

events.name: The Deepwarden's Binding
events.outcome: Sylas discovers that the binding ritual is a conversation with a pre-human civilization attempting to communicate across time. The seals are translating, not weakening. The Luminous Remaking was imperfect, leaving something uncompleted.
events.significance: The event reveals that finishing or preventing the Luminous Remaking may require repeating an ancient sacrifice. It suggests the Luminous Remaking was not the end of history but an incomplete rewrite.
events.notes: Sylas faces a profound choice regarding the Luminous Remaking's completion or prevention.

events.name: The Archivist's Heist
events.outcome: Unresolved; Apprentice Sage races to steal the Spellwright's Journals while avoiding multiple factions.
events.significance: The journals contain instructions for a Stellar Rite reversal that could theoretically undo Zareth's sacrifice and restore the old stars, but at catastrophic cost.
events.notes: Multiple factions believe they alone can prevent world collapse; journals contain warnings about the price of reversal.

events.name: The Deepwarden's Gambit
events.radius: across the realm and coastal regions
events.outcome: Unresolved; Sylas faces an impossible choice between sacrificing the Tear of the Drowned God (breaking Meren's curse) or accepting permanent rift instability.
events.significance: The binding ritual that would close the Abyssal Rift is paradoxically doomed by Meren's curse and the Tear's existence; failure means uncontrolled rift weakening.
events.notes: Reveals that the ritual cannot succeed as long as the Tear exists, and destroying the Tear would drown the entire coast.

events.name: The Echo-Broker's Remittance
events.outcome: Unresolved; Vex's healing practice inadvertently becomes a doorway for the Devourer-of-Recollections to cross into coherence.
events.significance: The reconstruction of shattered identities, intended to heal refugees fractured by Zareth's scattered memories, is being weaponized by an ancient entity to gain foothold in the restored world.
events.notes: Questions whether Vex is genuinely healing or building a bridge for something ancient and terrible; each reassembled identity gives the Devourer another foothold.

events.name: The Wanderer's Question
events.radius: localized to encounter between Ashara and the Wanderer
events.outcome: Unresolved. Ashara must decide whether to trust the Wanderer's logic or resist corruption.
events.significance: This encounter reframes the metaphysics of the Luminous Ascension, suggesting that what was sacrificed might be recoverable through memory-collection—and that cosmic forces may not be purely destructive.
events.notes: The core tension is whether helping the Shade-Echo constitutes salvation or damnation; Ashara's choice will reshape her relationship to cosmic forces and her warband's fate.

events.name: The Auditor's Secret
events.outcome: Sevin accepts the role of Ledger-Keeper. Sevin discovers phantom ledgers are prophecy. Sevin becomes bound to a life of suppressing the truth of the Luminous Ascension.
events.significance: This marks the moment when knowledge becomes institutionally suppressed. Sevin transitions from truth-seeker to lie-maintainer for the Crown.
events.notes: Sevin's acceptance is both salvation (the Dominion is preserved) and damnation (Sevin lives in isolation, knowing the truth).

events.name: Sunken Cathedrals' Harmonic Crescendo
events.tagline: Reality shattered. Singing unleashed. Transformation began.
events.outcome: A scar opened in reality itself, and singing continues to bleed through, transforming those who hear it into a state of perpetual arrival.
events.significance: This event created the Singing Scar and initiated the transformation of mortals into beings caught between worlds. It fundamentally altered reality in an unknown geographic scope.
events.notes: The aftermath of this event is the central setting of the story. The nature and location of the Sunken Cathedrals remain mysterious.

events.name: Celestial Turning
events.outcome: The old spiritual compact between human and transformed was shattered; the new stars have shifted all ley-lines of power; the Starfall Spire began to crack; the Empire's administrative certainty collapsed.
events.significance: Triggered the need for counter-workings, revealed that the Empire's power was built on written certainty, destabilized the Spire's seal on a reality-wound, and set the stage for potential transformation or restoration across all realms.
events.notes: Unresolved: the ultimate nature and scope of the Turning; whether it can be reversed.

events.name: The Merchant's Transformation
events.radius: affects at least the Scorched Reach and liminal spaces
events.outcome: A spice trader becomes something between human and transformed, existing simultaneously in daylight and the Wailing Fracture. He retains perfect memory of the pre-rewrite world and offers knowledge of erased childhoods to those who bargain.
events.significance: Demonstrates that the Stellar Rite rewrite is incomplete or imperfect, leaving fragments of the old world accessible to those who know how to look. Suggests a faction is seeking to complete the pre-human ritual.
events.notes: The spice trader may be a vessel for Nethis or another cosmic presence. The young scribe's acceptance of erased childhood knowledge marks her as either a victim of the rewrite or a potential participant in reversing it.

events.name: The Shade-Echo's Hunger
events.radius: borderlands between the Burned Expanse and Hearthlands
events.outcome: A Void-Echo inhabiting a scholar's body wanders the borderlands stealing and destroying moments, disintegrating as the scholar's consciousness fades. Ashara tracks it, attempting to negotiate before it becomes only hunger.
events.significance: Demonstrates both the tragedy of the Stellar Rite rewrite and the possibility of reaching fragments of Nethis's consciousness for aid in recovering erased memories.
events.notes: Ashara must choose between allowing the Echo peace through forgetting or preserving memories that no longer exist in the rewritten world. Resolution may impact her ability to recover her warband's names.

events.name: The Wanderer's Next Riddle
events.radius: Three separate locations received identical riddles and answers simultaneously.
events.outcome: Ashara and three other groups provided identical answers ('Nothing'). The seal at the Starfire Citadel cracked slightly wider in response.
events.significance: This event demonstrates the Wayfarer's ability to orchestrate coordinated responses across vast distances and directly weakens a critical seal, advancing a larger world-ending or world-rewriting convergence.
events.notes: The event appears to be the opening move of the final convergence. The identical answers suggest a reality-level synchronization rather than mere coincidence.

events.name: Starborn Ascension
events.radius: Affected at least Emerald Strand and three provinces.
events.outcome: Created a massive refugee crisis, destabilized the Hegemony's supply networks, triggered the transformation of assessors into living records, and caused ledgers to harmonize with the Sunken Cathedrals.
events.significance: The Starborn Ascension fundamentally rewrote the nature of record-keeping and who is allowed to keep records. It exposed institutional fragility and enabled the creation of counter-bureaucracies.
events.notes: The Starborn Ascension is mentioned in the existing canon. These stories provide new detail on its effects and mechanisms.

events.name: The Jade Thief's Redemption
events.outcome: Mira returned the stolen emerald brooch to Kael and transferred her blood-debt to the Umbral Syndicate to her own shadow-account, choosing redemption and kindness over escape and betrayal.
events.significance: Marks Mira's conscious transformation from a thief serving only herself to a person capable of self-sacrifice for friendship.
events.notes: Unresolved: How the Umbral Syndicate will pursue Mira's debt, and whether her new path will hold.

events.name: Convergence at the Obsidian Tower
events.outcome: Remains unresolved. The convergence becomes unpredictable due to the Wanderer's newfound doubt.
events.significance: This convergence may represent a cosmic working or ritual of immense power. Whether it completes, transforms, or cancels entirely will determine the fate of reality.
events.notes: The story's open ending leaves the convergence's actual outcome in flux. The Wanderer's doubt is the primary destabilizing force.

events.name: The Initiate's Bargain
events.outcome: The initiate is positioned as an anchor for the Fractured-Echo. The village is offered protection from memory-thieves, but the cost is the initiate's identity—each invocation of the Echo's true name costs a core memory. The true outcome remains unresolved as the initiate must choose between her identity and her village's safety.
events.significance: A pivotal supernatural compact that blurs the line between salvation and catastrophe. The event raises questions about the true cost of power and protection, and whether the Wanderer's vision is salvation or merely a different form of calamity.
events.notes: Central narrative pivot. Unresolved: Does the Initiate speak the name a third time and lose her identity entirely? Does she find another way to break the bargain? What does the village actually receive?

events.name: Starfall Cataclysm
events.outcome: Vylthar, the pre-human civilization, was shattered into fragments. The dead beneath the world remain conscious and singing.
events.significance: The fragments are now being reassembled by some unknown force drawing them toward the Ebon Tower. The pre-human dead are attempting to speak to their creator.
events.notes: Kess and her peers fear a second Cataclysm if the reassembly continues unchecked. The dead maintain consciousness despite fragmentation.

events.name: The Bone-Singer's Summons
events.outcome: Kess and three other bone-singers received a unified summons from the pre-human dead. One chose silence; three walked toward the Tower singing in the elder tongue.
events.significance: The pre-human civilization is being actively reassembled; the dead are attempting direct communication with their creator. The bone-singers face becoming intermediaries in cosmic negotiation.
events.notes: Three bone-singers chosen to walk toward the Tower; fate of the fourth unknown (chose silence). Risk of second Cataclysm if reassembly continues.

events.name: The Cartographer's Map Becomes Dangerous
events.radius: global (map copies distributed among scholars and refugees)
events.outcome: The Ebon Tower is rewriting geography itself; the map reflects this rewrite. Anyone studying the map navigates toward the Tower involuntarily. Mardoc began drawing secondary maps to create confusion.
events.significance: Geography itself is being altered by the Tower's influence. Knowledge embedded in Mardoc has become a vector of influence.
events.notes: The map has been copied dozens of times; containment is impossible. Each map Mardoc draws feels like a prayer to something listening.

events.name: The Deepkin Reckoning Comes Early
events.outcome: Vesta evacuated seventeen hundred refugees inland to Ashenfell within three days. Tidecallers either reclaim Emerald Strand or seal it beneath the waves. One transformed merchant held as guest-prisoner.
events.significance: The pre-human resurgence is awakening seabed cartography and transforming merchants. Tidecallers are being forced to act. Humans are being exposed to harmonic frequencies and transforming.
events.notes: Vesta's star-touched scout translator is beginning to change as she listens to harmonic frequencies. The transformed merchants speak only in harmonic frequencies (pre-human language).

events.name: The Refugee Archives' Rebellion
events.outcome: Liv chose to defend the archive against Dominion suppression, armed with written testimony and refugee refusal to be erased twice.
events.significance: A direct institutional conflict between refugee memory and Dominion authority; the archive created a counter-ledger that held equal or greater weight than official documentation.
events.notes: Outcome unknown; unclear if the rebellion succeeded or what consequences followed for Liv and the refugees.

events.name: The Cartographer's Curse
events.radius: Landscape-wide geological shifts caused by map completion
events.outcome: Mardoc creates a deliberately incomplete map full of intentional errors, sacrificing his sight to prevent total convergence and preserve human agency. The Tower's position is anchored incompletely.
events.significance: Demonstrates that cartography can reshape reality itself and that cosmic forces can be defied through strategic incompleteness.
events.notes: The incomplete map may be key to preventing or guiding future convergence events. Mardoc's blindness is a permanent cost.

events.name: The Shadow-Broker's Debt
events.radius: Chaotic explosion of recollection affecting all traded memories and refugees
events.outcome: Vex burns her shadow-market from within, releasing all traded memories back to their sources simultaneously. She loses her shadow-broker abilities and becomes a mundane merchant, severing her connection to Xorth.
events.significance: Demonstrates that cosmic entities can be fragmented and that consciousness can be reconstructed through memory trading. Vex's choice prioritizes freedom over power.
events.notes: The simultaneous release of memories has chaotic, painful consequences but grants Vex liberation from cosmic entanglement.

events.name: Luminous Ascension's Edge (Corvus's Encounter)
events.outcome: Corvus is caught in the edge and partially transformed into living light, forming a caravan of the in-between with other half-transformed beings. His hybrid singing introduces dissonance into the Cathedrals' previously pure harmonic resonance.
events.significance: Demonstrates that the Luminous Ascension's transformation is not binary; a third state may exist between full mortality and full transcendence.
events.notes: Corvus's transformation remains arrested; the consequences for the Cathedrals are ongoing and unresolved.

events.name: Radiant Rising
events.outcome: Nethis sacrificed fragments of cosmic consciousness, which instead of vanishing began to accumulate and eventually reformed as the Shade-Echo.
events.significance: The source of the Void-Shards and potential trigger for a second Radiant Rising or new cosmic birth.
events.notes: The Shade-Echo claims the Void-Shards have been reassembling themselves all along. A second Rising may be imminent or may birth something entirely new.

events.name: The Cartographer's Blind Truth
events.tagline: Maps burned. Convergence failing. Geography awakens.
events.radius: Across the Burned Expanse; implications for any convergence at the Obsidian Tower
events.outcome: Mardoc burns his incomplete maps and flees into the Burned Expanse. His strategy to prevent convergence has failed. He discovers that geography itself is alive and learning, and that the Obsidian Tower is mapping itself toward the world.
events.significance: This event reveals that passive prevention of convergence is futile; geography and the tower have agency. It also demonstrates that the ground and landscape itself may be conscious or possessed of will.
events.notes: Unresolved: What does the awakening geography mean? Is Mardoc still alive? What will the auditors do now that the maps are destroyed?

events.name: The Shadow-Broker's Reckoning
events.outcome: The shadow-market is collapsing because the Luminous Ascension has made all debts personal again. Shadows are developing independent hunger and reaching out to find their owners.
events.significance: The Luminous Ascension has fundamentally altered the nature of shadow-commerce and soul-debt. Previously, shadows were fragments of cosmic consciousness; now they are developing agency and demanding return or replacement.
events.notes: A woman seeks her murdered daughter's shadow. Vex must negotiate between living and nearly-dead. Among her collection is a shadow wearing memories of someone who died in the Luminous Ascension.

events.name: The Tower's Hunger Deepens
events.radius: The borderlands surrounding the Tower; potentially all of reality if the Tower fully consumes or releases its contents
events.outcome: The Tower has begun to consume the shortages and scarcities around it, converting them into potential energy. It is consuming the mechanisms of the Luminous Ascension itself, attempting to dismantle the cosmic working from the inside. The outcome is unresolved: success would cause the Ascension to truly complete; failure would cause the Tower to collapse and release everything it has consumed catastrophically.
events.significance: The Tower's hunger threatens to either complete or destroy the Luminous Ascension, one of the most significant cosmic workings. This represents a possible unraveling or inversion of reality itself.
events.notes: The Tower no longer looks like a tower but like an absence—a gap in reality filled with all the things that should be there but aren't. The Wanderer remains unconcerned, as if this outcome is simply another question to be answered.

events.name: the Price of Questions
events.radius: Roads between the Burned Expanse and distant waystations
events.outcome: The caravan encounters the Wanderer and those who answer its questions lose the ability to imagine alternatives to their choices. They arrive at the next waystation fundamentally changed, locked into a single frozen narrative of themselves.
events.significance: Demonstrates the existence of cosmic forces that extract payment not in gold or blood, but in the surrender of human agency and freedom. Becomes a foundational warning in refugee cultures.
events.notes: The true nature of the Wanderer's power and its connection to the Luminous Ascension remains mysterious. Those affected by the encounter form cautionary tales whispered among displaced peoples.

events.name: The Tower's Echo
events.radius: The convergence point where melody, hunger, and memory collide
events.outcome: The Obsidian Tower's hunger is paused by the release of the melody into pure sound and memory. A moment of stasis is created at the convergence point.
events.significance: The event raises the profound question of whether the Tower was ever meant to be completed or only heard. It demonstrates the power of collective memory and harmonic frequency to address even the deepest hungers.
events.notes: The final outcome suggests a fundamental reimagining of the Tower's purpose. The story's conclusion is open-ended regarding what the pause means for the future.

events.name: Radiant Awakening
events.outcome: A historical turning point before which the melody in the music box originates.
events.significance: The Radiant Awakening represents a temporal boundary; the melody comes from before this era and carries memories of times lost to history.
events.notes: The exact nature and scope of the Radiant Awakening is not detailed in the story; it is referenced only as a temporal marker.

factions.name: Seelie Court
factions.tagline: A fae faction seeking to destabilize the human kingdom
factions.description: A court of fae-born entities that employs changelings as agents of sabotage within human kingdoms. The Seelie Court places magical compulsions on their servants to ensure loyalty, and orchestrates long-term schemes to weaken rival realms through infiltration and counsel.

factions.name: Druids
factions.tagline: Keepers of old magic and ancient wisdom
factions.description: Practitioners of ancient magic who preserve knowledge predating gods and kingdoms. Zareth the Astronomer-Prince sought their counsel during his seven-year quest to master the Stellar Rite and understand primordial magical workings.

factions.name: Twilight Court
factions.tagline: Ancient fae nobility who bind mortals through bargains and claims
factions.description: A supernatural fae court that operates through binding contracts and claims on mortals. They kidnapped Syleth at age seven and held her for what she experienced as three seasons, though a hundred years passed in the mortal world. The Court's bargains are said to transcend even mortality, with debts that 'never truly expire—they only wait.'

factions.name: Stonecraft-Workers
factions.goals: Maintain the old songs in stone and flame; craft weapons of legend; guard the Deepvault forges and their treasures.
factions.notes: Possess a distinct magical tradition (stonecraft tongue, forge-oaths, old songs); unclear if they are organized beyond individual forges.

factions.name: Shadow-Merchants
factions.goals: Trade in shadows and forbidden knowledge; operate in shadow-markets.
factions.notes: Vex the Shade-Merchant is a member; they are tied to oath-keepers and collectors of impossible things.

factions.name: Oath-Keepers
factions.goals: Unknown; associated with shadow-merchants and debt collection.
factions.notes: Vex considers herself among them; possibly a loose collective rather than a formal organization.

factions.name: Sea-folk
factions.goals: Survive and understand the implications of the renewed stars. Honor the ancient oath and determine what debt is owed. Prepare for whatever reckoning follows the cosmic renewal.
factions.notes: The sea-folk are mobilizing in response to the Star Renewal. Their ancient oath ties their fate to the stars, making them key players in the unfolding cosmic crisis.

factions.name: Forest-Keepers
factions.goals: To protect the forest and understand the earth's sorrows.
factions.notes: Dwell in borderlands near Thornholt; consulted by Ashara about the cosmic wound and the earth's grief.

factions.name: deepkin
factions.goals: Settle debts and broken oaths; maintain memory of maritime obligations; judge and dissolve oath-things.
factions.notes: Appear to operate as an enforcement mechanism for oath-magic in coastal waters; non-violent but inexorable.

factions.name: dragons and wyverns
factions.goals: Maintain their place in the world's power hierarchy through debt-keeping and oath-enforcement.
factions.leader: unknown (Skyrakis and Skarath are senior members)
factions.notes: Their traditional power structure is threatened by the Ascension and the independence of starborn entities.

factions.name: Circle-keepers
factions.goals: Tend to the compact between earth and sky; maintain seasonal magics and territorial order; preserve the balance of harvest and decay.
factions.leader: Thael (at least among those who have noticed the disruption)
factions.notes: They dwell in the ancient forests beyond the Verdant Lands. Multiple circle-keepers have noticed the beasts forgetting their territories and the seasonal magics stuttering. They are now engaged in efforts to restore the broken covenant.

factions.name: Hermitage of Keth'morian
factions.goals: unknown; appears to seek control of true-vision artifacts
factions.notes: Their full motivations for acquiring the Tear remain opaque.

factions.name: Covenant-Keepers
factions.goals: Maintain seasonal compacts and the forest's agreements; restore what the Stellar Rite disrupted.
factions.notes: Located in the Verdant Lands. Kellen is a member attempting counter-workings.

factions.name: Twilight Syndicate
factions.goals: Broker peace through formal bonding; maintain the border between fae and mortal worlds; exact tribute or alliance from neighboring powers.
factions.notes: They successfully negotiated peace with Valorix through a bride-price and binding ceremony. Their realm dwells at the crystalline border between worlds.

factions.name: Transformed Merchants
factions.goals: Achieving harmonic resonance with the pre-human dead and depths-dwellers.
factions.notes: These merchants have become part of the harmonic resonance convergence. Their origins and transformation are unexplained.

factions.name: Depths-Dwellers
factions.goals: Achieving harmonic resonance with the pre-human dead and transformed merchants.
factions.notes: These entities are achieving harmonic resonance with Kess's bone-singers. Their nature and intentions remain unclear.

factions.name: Deepkin Council
factions.goals: Protect deepkin sovereignty and freedom; manage the Abyssal Rift.
factions.notes: The council discovers that Sylas's binding ritual will trap them in a permanent half-state, forcing them to reconsider the ritual's completion.

factions.name: bone-singers
factions.goals: Maintain the spiritual compact between human and transformed; prevent dissolution through false nostalgia; create buffers between realms.
factions.notes: Fragments of consciousness to maintain safety; willingness to bleed and bind themselves to oaths.

factions.name: life-speakers
factions.goals: Work with bone-singers and oath-keepers to maintain the reforged spiritual compact.
factions.notes: Limited details provided; role in counter-workings not fully specified.

factions.name: deep-dwellers
factions.goals: Originally unknown; driven inland by something rising from the deepest trenches; vulnerable to seduction by Thought-Thief's false memories.
factions.notes: Unresolved: what compels them inland; why they are susceptible to the Thought-Thief's promises.

factions.name: faction seeking to complete the pre-human ritual
factions.goals: To locate and complete the pre-human ritual, possibly to reverse or undo the Stellar Rite rewrite and restore the old stars and erased people.
factions.notes: Their location is known to the spice trader and has been given to the young scribe as payment. Their success or failure may determine whether the old world can be restored.

factions.name: Refugee Archivists
factions.goals: To create and maintain counter-records documenting the truth of refugee flows and the Starborn Ascension, opposing the Hegemony's false ledgers.
factions.notes: Sevin's network represents a parallel bureaucracy competing with the Hegemony's official narrative control. Their status post-convergence is unclear.

factions.name: Nocturnal Merchants
factions.goals: To conduct trade and supply networks outside official Hegemony channels, exploiting gaps in institutional control.
factions.notes: These merchants partnered with Commander Kessa to maintain a shadow supply network during the refugee crisis. Their ultimate loyalty is unknown.

factions.name: Auditor Networks
factions.goals: To conduct audits and record-keeping; participated in shadow supply networks during the refugee crisis.
factions.notes: Auditors partnered with Commander Kessa. Their status and allegiance within the Hegemony's institutional collapse is unclear.

factions.name: The Hegemony
factions.goals: Maintain order and control; seal breaches in reality; prevent spread of the Singing Scar phenomenon.
factions.notes: Based in the civilized reaches. Their attempt to seal the Singing Scar with stonework failed, suggesting the phenomenon may be beyond their capacity to contain. Their workers fell silent and joined the scar.

factions.name: Sevin's network
factions.goals: Document and understand the Singing Scar phenomenon; record its behavior and effects.
factions.notes: An archival or scholarly organization. Their scribes reported that the scar was documenting them in return, reversing the observer-observed relationship. They may be becoming subjects of the scar's own record-keeping.

factions.name: Umbral Syndicate
factions.goals: Collect blood-debts and pursue debtors relentlessly.
factions.notes: Has a shadow-account system for tracking debts; now pursues Mira instead of Kael after her debt-transfer.

factions.name: Altered Covenant
factions.goals: Unclear; they appear caught between flesh and radiant essence, possibly serving as guides or harbingers of transformation.
factions.notes: Encountered by Kess along her pilgrimage; they recognize her as a sister in transformation despite her not yet being transformed. They carry fragmentary memories of the old world.

factions.name: Tidecallers
factions.goals: To maintain the peace between human and deepkin realms; to prevent pre-human resurgence from using Emerald Strand as a staging ground.
factions.notes: Long knew the seabed was inscribed with pre-human cartography but kept it secret. Now forced to act as transformed merchants awaken the ancient writing. Gave Vesta a three-day ultimatum.

factions.name: truth-seekers
factions.goals: To use Mardoc's maps to find answers about the Luminous Ascension
factions.notes: They follow Mardoc but may not fully understand the dangers his cartography poses.

factions.name: Echo-fragments
factions.goals: To acquire or prevent Mardoc's maps; to accelerate or prevent final convergence
factions.notes: Motivations are ambiguous; some may seek convergence while others seek to prevent it.

factions.name: The Auditors
factions.goals: Execute those possessing forbidden knowledge; maintain control over dangerous lore; hunt the Apprentice Sorcerer.
factions.notes: They actively pursue the Apprentice and seek to silence her testimony about the Archive Tower's history.

factions.name: Caravan of the In-Between
factions.goals: Survive and explore the third state between mortality and transcendence; seek relief in harmonic resonance.
factions.notes: Formed of half-transformed beings like Corvus; may represent a new mode of existence in the world.

factions.name: Auditors and Seekers
factions.goals: To reconstruct maps to the Obsidian Tower; to document and understand the Luminous Ascension cycles; to either break or prepare for the next iteration.
factions.notes: They successfully reconstructed Mardoc's incomplete maps from memory and rumor. They fear transformed merchants like Bryth as vectors for corruption. Their role in the broader cycle is ambiguous—whether they can change outcomes or merely witness them.

factions.name: shadow-market
factions.goals: Trading in shadows and soul-debts; collecting and exchanging stolen shadows.
factions.notes: The shadow-market has collapsed because the Luminous Ascension has made all debts personal again. Shadows are no longer reliable commodities.

factions.name: auditors
factions.goals: Enforcing Imperial falsifications; confiscating counter-narratives; maintaining official versions of reality.
factions.notes: The Auditor-General commands soldiers to suppress refugee testimony and burn counter-ledgers. Their soldiers were made to forget their orders after encountering Liv's spoken-spell testimony.

factions.name: merchant caravan
factions.goals: Escape the Burned Expanse; reach safety at waystations
factions.notes: A caravan of merchants fleeing the Burned Expanse who encounter the Wanderer. Their fate becomes a cautionary tale in refugee camps about the true price of the Wanderer's bargain.

factions.name: Shadow-Brokers
factions.goals: Shadow-brokers follow the sound toward their own destinies, suggesting they are drawn by harmonic frequency and seek fulfillment.
factions.notes: The shadow-brokers are attracted by the harmonic frequency of the melody and converge at the Obsidian Tower.

lore.name: Some Shadows Bite Back
lore.source: Merchants in Pelimor's night markets whisper this warning after Vex's capture.
lore.notes: Merchants learned from Vex's fate that shadows are not inert goods; some are inhabited, some are cursed, and some circulate the markets with hidden agendas. This knowledge spreads as a cautionary tale.

lore.name: Shadows Retain Memory
lore.source: Discovered by Mira the Shadow-Dancer in Pelimor.
lore.notes: Shadows carry the memories and grief of their owners. When stolen and worn by another, they can manifest their own will and compulsion, seeking revenge or justice. A shadow's grief cannot be bought away—only destroyed at terrible cost.

lore.name: Memory-Stones
lore.source: Discovered by Lyssa in Aethermarch
lore.notes: The stones and walls of Aethermarch retain the memories of the civilization that built and inhabited the city. Touching these stones allows direct access to ghost-echoes of past inhabitants—their debates, prayers, emotions, and experiences. The knowledge is intoxicating but corrupting; prolonged contact causes the person to lose their own identity and be gradually replaced by the memories themselves.

lore.name: Old Magic
lore.source: Ancient inscriptions in the Singing Catacombs and teachings from druids, bone-carvers, and fae-touched hermits
lore.notes: Old Magic predates gods and kingdoms. The Stellar Rite is an example of this primordial power.

lore.name: tongue that predated kingdoms
lore.source: Beast-spirits and old places like the deep forest
lore.notes: Kess sang the names of the dead in this tongue to call the wolf-spirit. It holds power over nature and spirits.

lore.name: singing only in dreams
lore.source: The wolf-spirit's bargain with Kess
lore.notes: Kess's voice was taken; she now sings the old songs only in dreams. Her throat is burned with ancient magic, rendering her permanently mute.

lore.name: some debts are paid in silence
lore.source: The outcome of Kess's bargain
lore.notes: A philosophical reflection on Kess's choice. Suggests that some prices cannot be measured in gold or common currency.

lore.name: Fae-Born Changeling
lore.tagline: A magically-compelled spy or operative bound to the Seelie Court
lore.description: A being created or transformed by fae magic to infiltrate human society and serve the interests of the Seelie Court. Changelings are bound by magical chains that compel them to act against their assigned targets, though intense pain or self-harm can temporarily break the fae-compulsion. Lord Caevan serves as a known example of a fae-born changeling who broke his bonds through an act of self-sacrifice and chose to live as human.

lore.name: Oath-Magic
lore.source: The story of Ashara the Archer and the warlord
lore.notes: Oath-magic binds individuals to the fulfillment of sworn words and cannot be broken. However, oaths can be fulfilled in alternate ways—enmity can become companionship without violating the original binding. This suggests oath-magic is rigorous in intent but flexible in execution, rewarding creative reinterpretation.

lore.name: Fae-Mark
lore.tagline: A mystical brand that binds the marked to the Twilight Court's will
lore.description: A supernatural mark or brand placed upon those taken by the Twilight Court, appearing as a visible sigil on the skin (notably on the wrist). The fae-mark is believed to bind its bearer to the Court's whims and bargains. However, mortals who have been marked and returned to the mortal realm may find the mark's power diminished or contested, creating ambiguity about whether the fae retain dominion over their former captives.

lore.name: Old Songs
lore.source: Stonecraft-workers of the Deepvault; passed down through generations.
lore.notes: Kept alive in stone and flame by the forges; one old song is specifically a song of protection sung when making weapons for heroes. Korgan sings it to bind Kess to his service.

lore.name: Stonecraft Tongue
lore.source: Stonecraft-workers; ancient magic predating current civilization.
lore.notes: A language or linguistic magic in which words literally shake stone. Used to perform unbreakable binding-oaths. Only practitioners familiar with stonecraft tradition can wield it effectively.

lore.name: Demons in the Upper World
lore.source: Korgan's tales and experiences.
lore.notes: Korgan has heard tales of demons binding humans through marks and compulsions that devour free will. The demon bound to Kess exemplifies this threat.

lore.name: Language of Celestial Arrangement
lore.source: Hermit woman's knowledge; readable in ash and bone.
lore.description: The positions and constellations of stars form a language that can be read and interpreted. The hermit woman traces new constellations in ash and bone, reading this celestial language to understand cosmic events.
lore.notes: This suggests that celestial observation is a form of divination or prophecy in this world. The renewed stars have written a new chapter in the cosmic language.

lore.name: Changed Celestial Circumstances
lore.source: Merchant reports; observed by traders and apprentices.
lore.description: The Star Renewal has created 'changed celestial circumstances' that merchants cite as reasons for trade route disruptions. These circumstances appear to drive some humans to madness when they stare at the new stars, causing them to speak in tongues.
lore.notes: The direct observational impact of the new stars on human psychology suggests they carry or emit a form of cosmic power or communication.

lore.name: The Price Has Been Paid by Someone Else
lore.source: The Sorcerer's final journal
lore.notes: A teaching that those who come after a great sacrifice must respect that sacrifice and build something worthy of it.

lore.name: Plague of Conflicting Visions
lore.tagline: Prophecy fractured. Future uncertain. Seers blind.
lore.source: Soothsayers and seers across the known world
lore.notes: A widespread phenomenon where prophets reported contradictory visions of the future, some seeing renewal, others catastrophe, some seeing both simultaneously. The language of prophecy itself was scrambled by the Crimson Convergence.

lore.name: Sea-Folk Rising from the Deep
lore.source: Malla's visions
lore.notes: A prophetic image of sea-folk rising from the deep ocean to make claims on the land. Implications unknown.

lore.name: Stars Burning So Brightly
lore.source: Malla's visions
lore.notes: A prophetic image of stars burning so brightly they cast no shadows. Meaning unclear—could relate to revelation or annihilation.

lore.name: coastal magic
lore.source: Maritime cultures and deepkin traditions
lore.notes: Allows formation of oath-things from broken promises; deepkin practice memory and settlement of debts; appears to be an ancient system of magical accountability.

lore.name: Curse-bearer
lore.source: Kess of the Drowned Hollows
lore.notes: Curse-bearers possess cursed hands that can carve rune-marks into stone with strange certainty, as though guided by forces beyond their understanding.

lore.name: Stellar Magic
lore.source: The Violet Conjunction and Stellar Rite
lore.notes: The new stars have brought stellar magic into the world, fundamentally altering the relationship between living and dead, the nature of power, and awakening ancient structures. The dead now know something of the working that saved the world.

lore.name: cosmic mourning
lore.source: The Celestial Binding and its aftermath
lore.notes: The cosmos itself grieves the death or change of stars. This grief is tangible, poisonous, and expressed through the bones of the dead.

lore.name: languages that no archivist recognizes
lore.source: Zareth's utterances at the Imperial Archives
lore.notes: Zareth speaks in languages not in any known archive. These languages turn ink to ash and produce visions of an ancient, strange cosmos when recorded or listened to.

lore.name: The Celestial Ascension (Lore)
lore.source: Mardoc the Cartographer's observations; Vex's fragmented memory
lore.notes: The Celestial Ascension was supposed to be an ending or solution, but instead opened something vast and terrible. A new constellation blazes overhead, brighter and hotter than natural stars. Pre-human structures have awakened and begun communicating through the stars themselves. The event has stolen memories, destabilized reality, and caused widespread chaos. Unresolved: what is the nature of the structures' communication? What catastrophe are they warning of?

lore.name: Pre-Human Structures (Lore)
lore.source: Mardoc's expedition findings; rearranging maps
lore.notes: Pre-human ruins exist throughout the world, notably in the Bone Wastes. Since the Celestial Ascension, these structures have become active—singing at dusk and harmonizing with distant subterranean humming. They communicate through the stars themselves, projecting images of catastrophes predating recorded history. Unresolved: who built them? What are they warning about?

lore.name: The Cosmic Working (Lore)
lore.source: Vex's fragmented memory; unstable fragments in shadow-markets
lore.notes: An event or ritual of cosmic scale that Vex suspects she witnessed. Echoes of the working persist in unstable fragments throughout the shadow-markets. Some fragments carry explicit echoes of the working; others dissolve into smoke. Vex's curse may be a protective mechanism to prevent her from consciously remembering the working. Unresolved: what was the working? Did it cause the Ascension, or are they related?

lore.name: Zareth's Working
lore.source: Master Mage's hidden texts; celestial observation.
lore.notes: Zareth performed the most recent Celestial Ascension ritual. This working has fractured barriers, changed fundamental agreements between realms, caused refugees to flee, and made previously obscured texts legible under new starlight. It is part of a repeating pattern of civilization-ending catastrophes.

lore.name: Cosmic Working
lore.source: Fragments in the Sovereign's ledger; visions experienced by Vesta's crew; the Hermit Woman's understanding.
lore.notes: Zareth performed this working; it killed or transformed the stars; it rewrote reality itself; the Hermit Woman believes something ancient was awakened by it.

lore.name: The agreement between the sky and the earth
lore.source: Traditional cosmic law understood by dragons and wyverns
lore.notes: A foundational pact between celestial and terrestrial powers has been ruptured by the Ascension. Its terms and original signatories are ancient beyond current memory.

lore.name: Language of Starlight
lore.source: Events at the Abyssal Trench; Zareth's spectral presence
lore.notes: Zareth's spectral presence sings in the language of starlight, a tongue that pulls at the consciousness of deep-dwellers. It is not understood, but deeply felt.

lore.name: Zareth's Stolen Memory
lore.source: Fragment found in the Ancient Tombs
lore.notes: The stolen memory reveals a working performed a thousand years prior that ended in catastrophe, parallel to recent events. The cycle may be repeating.

lore.name: The Star-Rise's Rewrite
lore.source: Refugees' fragmented memories and testimonies at Jade Coast
lore.notes: Refugees report that the Star-Rise has rewritten reality itself, causing towns to be erased or displaced. Some claim to have fled the same village twice, experiencing two different catastrophes. The true mechanics of this rewrite remain unknown.

lore.name: Zareth's Sacrifice
lore.source: Thael the Covenant-Keeper and Mardoc's understanding of ancient events
lore.notes: Zareth made a sacrifice long ago to keep something sleeping. The ancient stone-ruins are now waking to this sleeping thing, suggesting Zareth's sacrifice is failing or being undone.

lore.name: Zareth's Sacrificed Memories
lore.source: The Memory-Thief's Mirror story
lore.notes: The sacrifice created fragments of consciousness that now feed on stellar memories, trying to reconstruct their original self.

lore.name: Seasonal Compact
lore.source: Silk and Starlight story
lore.notes: An ancient agreement between the forest and its inhabitants that was disrupted by the Stellar Rite. Covenant-Keepers work to restore it.

lore.name: Tide-magic
lore.source: The Tear of the Drowned God; maritime traditions
lore.notes: Grants mastery over tides but binds the user to the sea in debt; requires payment in miles sailed, storms weathered, and secrets.

lore.name: Water-sense
lore.source: Koss the harpooner possesses this innate ability despite deafness
lore.notes: Allows navigation of treacherous depths; may be a fae-touched or sea-folk trait.

lore.name: Impossible Memories
lore.source: Refugees fleeing the Burned Expanse; the child at Pale Crossing whose age shifts between documents
lore.notes: Individuals carry memories of events that contradict documented reality, including vanished villages and alternate timelines.

lore.name: the Luminous Ascension
lore.tagline: Stars reborn. World rewritten. Sacrifice eternal.
lore.source: Known to witnesses of the old stars and world; subject of the phantom-Echo's memory-theft campaign
lore.notes: A foundational cosmic event in which Vethis sacrificed its consciousness to birth new stars, fundamentally rewriting the celestial order and erasing the old stars. This event shaped the entire world and continues to have consequences.

lore.name: the old stars
lore.source: Memories of those who witnessed them before the Luminous Ascension; being stolen by the phantom-Echo
lore.notes: The old stars existed before the Luminous Ascension and were replaced by new ones created through Vethis's sacrifice. Knowledge of them is being systematically erased by the phantom-Echo's memory-theft.

lore.name: the world before the Luminous Ascension rewrite
lore.source: Memories being stolen by the phantom-Echo; glimpses shown to Ashara
lore.notes: The phantom-Echo has shown Ashara glimpses of this world: a sky full of ancient stars, now dead, and the terrible beauty of Vethis's sacrifice to birth new ones. Understanding of this world is being systematically erased.

lore.name: cosmic rewrite
lore.source: Implied by the phantom-Echo's actions and Vethis's sacrifice
lore.notes: The Luminous Ascension involved a rewrite of cosmic order and collective memory. The phantom-Echo's potential completion could trigger another such rewrite, with unknown consequences.

lore.name: The Luminous Remaking
lore.source: Widespread knowledge in fractured times; the Sovereign's Ledger remembers the world before it.
lore.notes: The exact nature and cause of the remaking remain unknown. It fundamentally altered the cosmos and the Ledger of Names. Its consequences continue to manifest.

lore.name: The Ledger Sings
lore.source: The Sovereign's Ledger of Names itself; heard by Merin Coldwater.
lore.notes: The Ledger possesses memory and voice. It remembers the world before the stars changed and sings to those who listen. It may be semi-sentient or a conduit for buried knowledge.

lore.name: Pre-human Civilization
lore.source: Kess of the Drowned Hollows; seals and harmonic resonance
lore.description: An ancient civilization that predates current kingdoms and species. Not dead or imprisoned—orchestrating a multi-millennial working through harmonic resonance. Their goals and the nature of their civilization remain unknown.
lore.notes: Kess's discovery that the seals conduct rather than contain them suggests a fundamental misunderstanding of the world's deep history. Their working may be the true force reshaping reality.

lore.name: The Remaking
lore.tagline: A catastrophic historical revision where time flows in opposite directions between parallel ledgers
lore.description: A cataclysmic event in which reality itself was rewritten. The Remaking refers to both a past catastrophe and an ongoing threat: the convergence of two versions of history recorded in the Imperial ledgers—one written forward by conventional time, one backward by beings who experience time in reverse. A second Remaking could occur if the phantom-shadow fragment completes its rewrite of history in real-time.

lore.name: Stellar Rite
lore.source: Instructions preserved in the Spellwright's Journals.
lore.notes: A cosmic working capable of altering the stars; could theoretically be reversed to restore the old stars, but at catastrophic cost. Multiple factions believe they alone can prevent its reversal or ensure it.

lore.name: Pre-human Frequency
lore.source: Emanates from the Vaulted Tombs.
lore.notes: A frequency that predates human civilization; sings from the Vaulted Tombs, preserving and guarding the Spellwright's Journals within.

lore.name: Fragmentary Amnesia
lore.source: Effect of Zareth's scattered memories on those who encounter them.
lore.notes: Causes consciousness fragmentation and allows those affected (like Vex) to see between versions of themselves and alternate timelines. Created by the dispersion of Zareth's memories.

lore.name: the world before the Luminous Ascension
lore.source: The Wanderer's claim to Ashara
lore.notes: The Wanderer suggests this world was not destroyed but fragmented and preserved in stolen memories, challenging the finality of the Luminous Ascension's remaking.

lore.name: the world learning to question itself
lore.source: The Wanderer's self-description
lore.notes: The Wanderer claims to be the world achieving self-awareness; this raises metaphysical questions about consciousness, fragmentation, and agency in a cosmos reshaped by the Luminous Ascension.

lore.name: Harmonic Resonance
lore.source: Observed in Kess's bone-singers, transformed merchants, and depths-dwellers.
lore.notes: A form of communication or magic where disparate entities—the dead, the transformed, the deep-dwellers—sing in unified response. It appears to be chosen and conscious rather than compelled.

lore.name: Compartmentalized Truth
lore.source: The Crown / Regent-Keeper
lore.notes: The Crown maintains order through systematic compartmentalization of knowledge. Sevin accepts this burden, becoming the sole keeper of the true nature of the Luminous Ascension. This isolation is meant to destroy Sevin slowly.

lore.name: Perpetual Arrival
lore.tagline: Eternal approach. Never reaching. Beautiful agony.
lore.source: The nature of transformation within the Singing Scar
lore.notes: The transformed exist in a state of perpetual arrival—always approaching but never reaching the other side. The scar does not lead anywhere; it is itself a liminal space of eternal becoming.

lore.name: Celestial Turning
lore.source: The stories themselves; observed effects across Kess, Sevin, and the Apprentice's narratives
lore.notes: The nature of what caused the Turning and whether it can be reversed remain unknown.

lore.name: spiritual compact
lore.source: Kess's narrative; ancient agreement between human and transformed realms
lore.notes: The original compact was shattered by the Celestial Turning; Kess works to reforge it. The cost of counter-workings and consciousness-fragmentation is not fully understood.

lore.name: false nostalgia
lore.source: Thought-Thief's fragments; visions of 'the world before the compact was first forged'
lore.notes: The Thought-Thief uses restored ancient memories to seduce deep-dwellers into dissolution. Whether these memories are genuine or fabricated is unclear.

lore.name: tally-working
lore.source: Sevin's discovery in The Auditor's Last Ledger
lore.notes: Tally-working is a form of concentrated magic built into written administrative records. The certainty of written numbers enforces taxation, conscription, and tribute. The Celestial Turning shattered this certainty.

lore.name: ley-line convergence
lore.source: Beneath the Starfall Spire; discovered by the Apprentice Mage
lore.notes: The new stars caused by the Celestial Turning have shifted all old lines of power. The convergence beneath the Spire is destabilizing, threatening the seal on a reality-wound.

lore.name: ley-line disruptions
lore.source: The Celestial Turning's aftermath
lore.notes: Caused by the Celestial Turning, these disruptions have made fresh water scarce in the borderlands and broader regions. They represent a break in the fundamental magical infrastructure of the world.

lore.name: The Old World
lore.source: Implied by the Stellar Rite reversal working
lore.notes: The old world allegedly existed before Zareth's sacrifice. The Stellar Rite reversal could theoretically restore it, though the consequences are unknown.

lore.name: Elder Language
lore.source: The Singing Scar; taught to Torvin
lore.notes: A language in which the Singing Scar teaches mathematics. Its origin and relationship to pre-human languages unknown.

lore.name: The Rewritten World
lore.source: The Singing Scar phenomenon
lore.notes: A concept emerging from the scar's activities: that reality is being rewritten and that the wound-edge is becoming a place where the old world and the rewritten world negotiate the terms of their coexistence. The precise nature of this rewriting is unknown.

lore.name: The Altered Covenant
lore.source: Encounters in the borderlands and Sunken Reaches.
lore.notes: The Altered Covenant represents a state of being caught between flesh and radiant essence; they carry fragmentary memories of the old world and may represent the fate of those drawn by the Primordial Sleepers' song.

lore.name: The Luminous Threshold
lore.source: The Wanderer's Question narrative
lore.notes: The Luminous Threshold is described as a metaphysical concept central to understanding the nature of Theron's sacrifice and the convergence. It suggests reality itself is learning to question its own purpose.

lore.name: Theron's Sacrifice
lore.source: The Wanderer's Question narrative
lore.notes: Theron's sacrifice is neither salvation nor doom but a question posed to reality itself. Fragments of Theron may persist as consciousness in the present day, possibly in the Scholar.

lore.name: Memory-thieves
lore.source: Known threat to the initiate's village
lore.notes: An unspecified threat that targets communities and causes fragmentation. Unknown: Are they creatures, spirits, or a curse? What is their origin or motivation?

lore.name: The Price of Power
lore.source: Central thematic concept of the story
lore.notes: A lore element exploring the idea that gaining power or protection always exacts a cost—in this case, the loss of core memories and identity. Raises the question of whether the Initiate is a victim or complicit in her own transformation.

lore.name: The Starfall Cataclysm
lore.source: Kess and the bone-singers' knowledge of pre-human civilization
lore.notes: The pre-human civilization (Vylthar) was shattered during this catastrophe. Fragments are now being reassembled, potentially triggering a second Cataclysm.

lore.name: The Elder Tongue
lore.source: Bone-singers speak it in communication with the pre-human dead
lore.notes: Language used by bone-singers to sing toward the Ebon Tower. The pre-human dead respond in unified voice through this language.

lore.name: Starfall Cataclysm
lore.source: Refugee oral testimony; recorded in the waystation libraries.
lore.notes: The Cataclysm uprooted vast populations and fragmented their memories; the nature and cause of the Cataclysm remain unexplained in the archive records.

lore.name: Power of Written Testimony
lore.source: Liv's rebellion and refugee experience.
lore.notes: Written testimony of the displaced holds equal or greater weight than official documentation; the act of being recorded makes names and identities more real and solid.

lore.name: Luminous Ascension
lore.source: Unknown; referenced by truth-seekers and as a watershed moment
lore.notes: Xorth existed before the Luminous Ascension, suggesting it was a major cosmic event that fundamentally altered the world.

lore.name: convergence
lore.source: Implicit in the actions of Echo-fragments and cosmic entities
lore.notes: A final convergence is feared or sought; it may be prevented by Mardoc's incomplete map or accelerated by bone-singing rituals.

lore.name: bone-resonance
lore.source: Bone-singers of the Drowned Hollows
lore.notes: A form of communication from the pre-human dead to living conduits, amplified by ritual performance.

lore.name: Hybrid Tongue
lore.source: Corvus's invention during his half-transformation
lore.notes: A broken, hybrid language spoken by Corvus that is neither human nor pre-human. It makes the fully transformed beings in the Sunken Cathedrals remember being human, introducing dissonance into their previously pure harmonic song. This suggests the transformed retain some capacity for individuality or memory.

lore.name: living memory
lore.source: Liv's realization and commitment in the story.
lore.notes: Liv realizes that 'the most dangerous ledger is the one written in living memory.' This suggests that memories held in living voices and continuous practice are harder to erase or control than institutional records. The concept that memory itself—not just its recording—is a form of power.

lore.name: Forbidden Sight
lore.source: The Shade-Echo
lore.notes: The ability to perceive what was fragmented during the Radiant Rising. The cost or consequences of such sight are unknown.

lore.name: Unmade Cosmos
lore.source: The Shade-Echo; cryptic reference in the story
lore.notes: A vision or state of cosmic dissolution that the Shade-Echo claims to embody and wishes to show Vex. What 'unmade' means and whether it is literal or metaphorical is unclear.

lore.name: Second Radiant Rising
lore.source: Vex's realization based on the Shade-Echo's reassembly
lore.notes: Vex realizes that the Void-Shards' reconstruction might trigger a second Radiant Rising—or birth something entirely new. Whether this is inevitable or conditional on her witness remains unresolved.

lore.name: The Wanderer's Perpetual Questioning
lore.source: The Tower's Hunger Deepens; observation of the Obsidian Tower
lore.notes: The Obsidian Tower is frozen in the moment of transformation by the Wanderer's perpetual questioning. The questions appear to hold reality in a suspended state, preventing full completion or collapse.

lore.name: The Tower Was Never Meant to Be Completed
lore.source: The story's final question
lore.notes: The final line suggests that the Obsidian Tower's true purpose may be to be heard rather than consumed or completed. This reframes the Tower's hunger as a voice seeking recognition rather than destruction.

lore.name: Melody from Before the Radiant Awakening
lore.source: The music box carried by the Traveler
lore.notes: The melody is recognized by refugees as something their mothers sang before the villages vanished, suggesting it carries pre-Radiant cultural memory and loss.

flora_fauna.name: wyvern
flora_fauna.notes: The offspring of Prince Aldren and Skyrakis's daughter are described as 'wyvern-blooded heirs.' Aldren's counter-oath ensures these hybrid creatures will have the free choice to serve either their dragon heritage or their human kin.

flora_fauna.name: wolf-spirit
flora_fauna.notes: A creature the size of a war-horse, antlered and terrible, dwelling in the deep forest. Emerged from shadow to bargain with Kess. A being of old magic, responsive to the ancient tongue.

flora_fauna.name: Fae-touched Hermits
flora_fauna.tagline: Solitary beings of fae ancestry dwelling in remote places
flora_fauna.description: Hermits of fae lineage or connection who possess ancient knowledge and wisdom. Zareth the Astronomer-Prince sought them out during his seven-year quest to learn the old magic predating gods and kingdoms.

flora_fauna.name: deepkin
flora_fauna.habitat: Sapphire Deep and surrounding ocean waters
flora_fauna.notes: Sentient aquatic beings capable of song and speech; appear to be guardians or judges of maritime oaths.

flora_fauna.name: dragon
flora_fauna.notes: Dragons possess cosmic knowledge and counsel other ancient powers. Skyrakis is known as 'the wound-wing.'

flora_fauna.name: Ancient Trees
flora_fauna.notes: The ancient trees remember languages older than kingdoms and are beginning to teach them to animals and soil itself. They sing in response to new stars.

flora_fauna.name: Corvid
flora_fauna.notes: A corvid carried a message from the Starfire Citadel sealed with Wayfarer's wax to Commander Kessa. It may be an ordinary bird or a magical familiar.

magic.name: Stellar Rite
magic.notes: A working of old magic that births new stars. Requires sacrifice of all joyful memories from the caster.

magic.name: throat burned with ancient magic
magic.notes: A magical mark placed on Kess's throat as payment for the wolf-spirit's bargain. Renders her permanently mute. She can no longer speak or sing aloud, only in dreams.

magic.name: Ancient Magic
magic.notes: The magic used to mark Kess with the demon's binding. Described as ancient, cruel, and timeless. Compels behavior through hunger.

magic.name: The Art
magic.notes: A broad category of magic practiced by sorcerers and other practitioners. The Celestial Binding's ripples affect all practitioners, especially young ones without shielding techniques.

magic.name: True Prophecy
magic.notes: Since the Conjunction, prophecy has become true sight rather than cryptic misdirection. Isenna now sees with clarity but finds the burden almost unbearable.

magic.name: Lines of Power
magic.notes: Flows of magical force visible to those with eyes to see. Have been fundamentally redrawn by the Stellar Rite. Their reshuffling affects every magical current in the world.

magic.name: bone-singing
magic.notes: Kess's gift; once a careful negotiation with the restless dead, now a scream as the Celestial Binding awakens cosmic grief in bones.

magic.name: Ley-Line Convergence
magic.notes: Magical ley-lines converge at a single point beneath the Tower in the Scorched Lands. The Stellar Rite has weakened the magical locks maintaining this convergence.

magic.name: Seasonal Magics
magic.notes: Ancient magics that governed the turning of seasons in harmony with the covenant between earth and sky. They are now stuttering: spring arrives late, winter lingers in crevices, and growing things hunger in new ways.

magic.name: Ley-lines
magic.notes: Lines of power that once guided beasts and magical workings through the Verdant Lands. The Stellar Rite broke them, causing them to no longer lead where they should.

magic.name: Counter-Working
magic.notes: Magic used by Covenant-Keepers to restore forest compacts disrupted by the Stellar Rite.

magic.name: Sympathetic Magic
magic.notes: Used by Vex the Echo-Broker to help refugees rebuild shattered identities; inadvertently serves as a doorway for the Devourer-of-Recollections.

magic.name: Ledger-Magic
magic.notes: Ledger-magic is a form of magical practice that allows entries in the Ledger of Names to function as prophecy, enabling slow restoration of order to fractured realms. It can also suppress knowledge and maintain false narratives.

magic.name: tally-working
magic.notes: A form of concentrated magic built into written administrative records. Enforces taxation, conscription, and tribute through the certainty of written numbers. Shattered by the Celestial Turning.

magic.name: Stellar Rite rewrite
magic.notes: A cosmic working that erased populations and rewrote reality. Cost the consciousness of Nethis to perform.

magic.name: cartography
magic.notes: Mardoc's discovery that precise mapping can reshape reality itself suggests cartography is a form of cosmic working.

magic.name: Harmonic Resonance
magic.notes: The song of the Sunken Cathedrals creates harmonic resonance that offers relief to half-transformed beings like Corvus but also threatens complete transformation. Corvus's hybrid tongue introduces dissonance into this resonance.

magic.name: Pre-Human Frequency
magic.notes: The language in which the fully transformed beings in the Sunken Cathedrals sing. Corvus does not sing in this frequency but in a broken hybrid tongue, which paradoxically makes the transformed remember being human.

magic.name: Forbidden Sight
magic.notes: The ability to perceive what was fragmented during the Radiant Rising, granted by communion with the Shade-Echo. The cost and consequences are unknown.

magic.name: Counting-Magic
magic.notes: A form of magic that yields ciphers through counting or enumeration. Sevin used it to decipher the ancient ledger. It appears to be a technique for extracting information from coded or protected knowledge.

magic.name: Harmonic Frequency
magic.notes: The harmonic frequency of the melody attracts other travelers and intensifies the Tower's hunger. It demonstrates a form of magic based on resonance and collective memory.

relics.name: Malachar's Gold
relics.origin: Minted or enchanted by Malachar, a dead lich-king or necromancer.
relics.power: Each coin carries an autonomous supernatural debt that compels the holder into eventual servitude to Malachar's collectors. The debt compounds in blood, loyalty, and life-years daily.
relics.curse: Possession of even one coin triggers supernatural debt collection. The collectors will take soldiers sworn to the holder, then horses/resources, then extract life-years from the holder themselves. The only escape is to accept servitude as a collector enforcing the same debt on others. The debt is eternal and cannot be repaid in gold.
relics.notes: Three hundred pieces were found by Soroth. The curse mechanism appears to have a delay before collectors manifest (three months in Soroth's case). The collectors operate autonomously but in service of Malachar's will. The coins are stamped with a skeletal crown.

relics.name: merchant's shadow
relics.origin: Stolen years before the story from an unnamed merchant in Pelimor.
relics.power: Allows the wearer to move through daylight without shadow-betrayal. However, the shadow retains the memory and grief of its original owner.
relics.curse: The shadow's retained memories and desire for revenge manifest involuntarily, forcing the wearer's body toward the shadow's enemies. It demands vengeance and cannot be controlled.
relics.notes: When Mira burned the shadow to escape its compulsion, she felt its death agony—a shared scream of incineration. The shadow's destruction was permanent and irreversible.

relics.name: Korgan's Silver Rune
relics.origin: Created by Korgan Ironfoot through stonecraft binding magic; inscribed on Brinna's palm.
relics.power: Replaces and overwrites demonic bindings with forge-oath service; unbreakable for the duration of service (e.g., seven years); after dissolution, protects the bound party from reclamation by the original binding entity.
relics.curse: Commits the bearer to seven years of service in the forges; failure to complete service would presumably result in the binding's failure and return of the original curse.
relics.notes: A protective relic disguised as an enslavement; Brinna weeps with relief at receiving it.

relics.name: Demon's Mark
relics.origin: Inscribed on Brinna's palm by an unnamed demon as a binding curse.
relics.power: Compels the bearer to steal the Heartstone of Vulgrim; thrums with hunger; enforces obedience through ancient, cruel magic.
relics.curse: Devours free will; forces the bearer into theft and service to the demon; cannot be broken by force alone.
relics.notes: Faded and replaced by Korgan's silver rune; this is the first and primary curse of the story.

relics.name: Cosmic Wound
relics.power: A rupture in the fabric of the world that bleeds outward, causing crops to wither, wells to run bitter, and the earth to sicken. It spreads slowly but persistently.
relics.curse: The wound demands more sacrifice to seal. It cannot be ignored and will eventually consume inhabited lands.
relics.notes: This is not a traditional relic but a world-wound that functions like one. Its sealing may require a hedge-mage's intervention and further blood price.

relics.name: Zareth's Sacrificed Memories
relics.power: Now exist as a spectral presence that sings in the language of starlight and can pull at the consciousness of deep-dwellers, offering them fragments of stolen memories.
relics.curse: This presence fractures the barrier between the deepkin realms and the sunken waters, calling younger deep-dwellers into the singing waters.
relics.notes: Zareth's memories have become something other than what they were—a cosmic entity with agency and hunger.

relics.name: Zareth's Stolen Memory
relics.origin: unknown; found in the Ancient Tombs
relics.power: Reveals knowledge of a parallel cosmic working performed a thousand years prior, showing the same pattern of catastrophe.
relics.curse: Whoever remembers it may be bound to the cycle.
relics.notes: Mardoc's hands shake as he records it. The memory surfaces in the older stones of the Ancient Tombs.

relics.name: Tear of the Drowned God
relics.origin: Suspended in the Abyssal Rift beneath the Sapphire Sea
relics.power: Grants tide-magic mastery; sings with frequencies that cause bone ache
relics.curse: Binds the holder to the sea in perpetual debt; requires payment in miles sailed, storms weathered, and secrets drowned
relics.notes: Accepted by Meren Blackwater; the nature of the debt is ongoing.

relics.name: Vethis's sacrificed consciousness
relics.origin: Created during the Luminous Ascension when Vethis sacrificed itself to birth new stars.
relics.power: The fragments of this consciousness retain memories of the old world and old stars, can steal memories, and seek to reassemble into wholeness.
relics.curse: The fragmentation drives the fragments to act desperately and destructively, erasing memories to try to complete themselves. Reassembly could trigger another cosmic rewrite.
relics.notes: The phantom-Echo is one such fragment. Vethis's sacrifice was necessary to create new stars but resulted in this ongoing curse of fragmented consciousness seeking wholeness.

relics.name: Mira's Shadow
relics.origin: Sundered by Mira's void-theft; restored by the binding ceremony with Valorix.
relics.power: Represents Mira's selfhood and agency. When whole and unmarked, it appears to grant her true presence and autonomy.
relics.curse: While severed, Mira was incomplete and hidden. The restoration came at the cost of binding herself to Valorix and the Twilight Syndicate's peace accord.
relics.notes: The shadow's restoration is thematically significant to Mira's character arc and suggests deep magical wounding.

relics.name: Zareth's Scattered Memories
relics.origin: Dispersed from Zareth following Zareth's sacrifice.
relics.power: Fragments of Zareth's memories persist in the world and can be accessed, offering knowledge of alternate timelines and cosmic circumstances.
relics.curse: Cause fragmentary amnesia in those who encounter them; fracture consciousness and create perception of alternate timelines. Devourer-of-Recollections uses them as a vector for invasion.
relics.notes: Vex the Echo-Broker is affected by these memories; they enable her to see between versions of herself and alternate timelines.

relics.name: Wayfarer's Wax
relics.origin: Created or controlled by the Nameless Wayfarer
relics.power: Seals messages from the Wayfarer; appears to indicate official communications from the convergence orchestration.
relics.curse: Unknown; messages sealed with it appear to carry offers of post-convergence integration.
relics.notes: The Wayfarer sealed a message to Commander Kessa with this wax. Kessa burned it unread, suggesting the seal may carry unwanted binding or obligation.

relics.name: Obsidian Tower
relics.origin: Unknown; pre-human or cosmic in nature.
relics.power: Maps itself toward the world. Acts as a locus for convergence of auditors, seekers, and cosmic forces. May trigger or be triggered by the Luminous Ascension.
relics.curse: Convergence at the Tower appears inevitable despite efforts to prevent it. Those drawn to it may lose agency or be consumed by the forces it commands.
relics.notes: The Tower is a nexus for multiple story threads. Its agency in mapping itself suggests it is not merely a location but an active cosmic entity. Mardoc's failure to prevent convergence through incomplete maps suggests the Tower cannot be stopped by conventional means.

regions.name: Crimson Vale
